alignments.pub_claim_text: Protein R methylation modulates protein-RNA interactions and suggests a role in rewiring protein-protein interactions.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the preprint's mechanistic conclusion about R methylation effects on RNA and protein interactions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: R methylation correlates differently with S/T-Y phosphorylation in response to various stimuli.
alignments.pub_claim_text: R methylation correlates differently with S/T-Y phosphorylation in response to various stimuli.
alignments.rationale: The correlation patterns between R methylation and phosphorylation reported in the preprint are carried forward unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Novel histone methylation marks on Serine 28 and Threonine 32 of H3 were successfully identified using hmSILAC.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Novel histone methylation marks on serine 28 and threonine 32 of H3 were successfully identified.
alignments.rationale: The novel histone methylation identifications are directly preserved in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Functional Equivalence Classes (FECs) are widespread across modalities and methods.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Functional Equivalence Classes (FECs) are identified as subsets of annotated and unannotated genes that jointly drive performance in gene-centric prediction tasks.
alignments.rationale: The preprint's broad statement that FECs are widespread is refined in the publication to a specific characterization as subsets driving predictive performance, narrowing and clarifying the original claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: FECs suggest that B cell markers can be decomposed into shared primary markers and tissue-specific secondary markers.
alignments.pub_claim_text: FECs suggest that B cell markers can be decomposed into shared primary markers and tissue-specific secondary markers.
alignments.rationale: The claim about decomposition of B cell markers is preserved verbatim in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: FECs are compatible with a wide range of functional encodings, with marker sets spanning at most 5% of the genome.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Functional Equivalence Classes (FECs) are identified as subsets of annotated and unannotated genes that jointly drive performance in gene-centric prediction tasks.
alignments.rationale: The preprint's quantitative and compatibility statements are absorbed into the publication's more general definition of FECs as driving subsets, which narrows the original scope and removes the explicit 5% quantification.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The identification of FECs in performance curves paves the way for novel functional characterization and increased robustness in analysis.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The identification of FECs in performance curves paves the way for novel functional characterization and increased robustness in the definition of functional gene sets.
alignments.rationale: The publication keeps the forward-looking claim but slightly reframes the robustness statement toward defining functional gene sets, clarifying the intended impact.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Early changes in mitochondrial membrane potential (Δψm) and in the production of mitochondrial Reactive Oxygen Species (mROS) are associated with macrophages that will later support bacterial growth.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Early changes in mitochondrial membrane potential (Δψm) and in the production of mitochondrial Reactive Oxygen Species (mROS) are associated with macrophages that will later support bacterial growth.
alignments.rationale: The experimental association reported in the preprint is preserved verbatim in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We achieved 83% accuracy in predicting L. pneumophila replication in single, infected cells before bacterial replication starts.
alignments.pub_claim_text: We achieved 83% accuracy in predicting L. pneumophila replication in single, infected cells before bacterial replication starts.
alignments.rationale: The predictive accuracy result is unchanged between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Backtracking is a valuable tool to gain new insights in host-pathogen interactions.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Backtracking is a valuable tool to gain new insights in host-pathogen interactions.
alignments.rationale: The conceptual claim about the utility of backtracking is retained without substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Early mitochondrial alterations are key predictive markers of the success of bacterial infection.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Early mitochondrial alterations are key predictive markers of the success of bacterial infection.
alignments.rationale: The conclusion that early mitochondrial changes predict infection success is unchanged in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The geometrically-embedded model with random connectivity can produce localized, finely spaced discrete attractors that effectively span a 2D manifold.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The model accomplishes spatial memory via attractor dynamics without synaptic fine-tuning or regular structure.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the attractor-based spatial memory idea but omits the specific claim about finely spaced localized attractors spanning a 2D manifold, reducing specificity and thus tempering confidence in the exact original claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The set of attracting states in the model can reliably encode a representation of spatial locations at which the system receives external input.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The set of attracting states can reliably encode a representation of the spatial locations at which the system receives external input.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same result about attracting states encoding spatial input locations with essentially identical wording and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The network's storage capacity statistics are equivalent to a full tiling of the plane, achievable without synaptic fine-tuning or regular structure.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The network's storage capacity statistics are equivalent to a full tiling of the plane.
alignments.rationale: The publication presents the same capacity-statistics claim; the meaning and scope are preserved.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This model may be relevant for understanding biological phenomena such as visuospatial working memory in two dimensions.
alignments.pub_claim_text: This model may be of interest in modeling biological phenomena such as visuospatial working memory in two dimensions.
alignments.rationale: The publication makes the same biological relevance claim but with higher stated certainty, clarifying and strengthening the contribution.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Single-vessel fMRI slow oscillations are directly coupled to brain state changes.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The RNN-based predictive modeling of rs-fMRI datasets reveals brain state-specific characteristics.
alignments.rationale: The publication frames the finding in terms of RNN-based modeling revealing brain-state characteristics, losing the preprint's explicit claim of direct coupling between single-vessel oscillations and brain-state changes, which constitutes a semantic revision and a tempering of the original mechanistic claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: An echo-state network (ESN) can predict the future temporal evolution of the rs-fMRI slow oscillatory feature from both rodent and human brains.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The RNNs trained with vessel-specific rs-fMRI signals encode the unique brain oscillatory dynamic feature.
alignments.rationale: The publication generalizes from ESNs to RNNs trained on vessel-specific signals and presents encoding of oscillatory features; this broadens the class of models while preserving the core predictive/encoding claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: rs-fMRI signals from individual blood vessels that are strongly correlated with neural calcium oscillations can be used to train an ESN for predicting brain state-specific rs-fMRI signal fluctuations.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The RNNs trained with vessel-specific rs-fMRI signals encode the unique brain oscillatory dynamic feature.
alignments.rationale: The publication keeps the idea that vessel-specific signals can be used to learn oscillatory features but omits the explicit linkage to neural calcium oscillations and frames it at higher certainty, thus revising specifics and amplifying confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The ESN-based prediction model classified variance-independent brain states based on global fluctuations of rs-fMRI features.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The RNN prediction method presents a unique data-driven encoding scheme to specify potential brain state differences based on the global fMRI signal fluctuation.
alignments.rationale: Both preprint and publication describe a data-driven RNN/ESN scheme that distinguishes brain-state differences from global fMRI fluctuations with similar emphasis and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The ESN revealed brain states with global synchrony and decoupled internal correlations within the default-mode network.
alignments.pub_claim_text: There is an inverse relationship between the global rs-fMRI signal fluctuation and the internal default-mode network (DMN) correlation.
alignments.rationale: The publication expresses the same finding (inverse relation between global fluctuations and DMN internal correlation) with higher certainty, so the semantic content is preserved and confidence is amplified.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The computational method χ -SCNN predicts the sources of Hi-C identified interactions at a high resolution of 100bp.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The computational method χ-CNN predicts the sources of Hi-C-identified interactions at a high resolution of 100 bp.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same methodological result about 100 bp resolution prediction with equivalent certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: χ -SCNN predictions recover original Hi-C peaks after extending them to be coarser.
alignments.pub_claim_text: χ-CNN predictions recover original Hi-C peaks after extending them to be coarser.
alignments.rationale: The recovery-of-peaks result is preserved verbatim between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: χ -SCNN predictions enrich for evolutionarily conserved bases, eQTLs, and CTCF motifs, supporting their biological significance.
alignments.pub_claim_text: χ-CNN predictions enrich for evolutionarily conserved bases, eQTLs, and CTCF motifs, supporting their biological significance.
alignments.rationale: The publication asserts the same enrichment result but assigns it higher certainty, representing an amplification of confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: χ -SCNN provides an approach for analyzing important aspects of genome architecture and regulation at a higher resolution than previously possible.
alignments.pub_claim_text: χ-CNN provides an approach for analyzing important aspects of genome architecture and gene regulation at a higher resolution than previously possible.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the methodological/conclusion claim about enabling higher-resolution analysis of genome architecture and regulation with similar emphasis and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Stacked contractive autoencoders can be effectively used as an unsupervised approach to preprocess infrared microscopic pixel spectra.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Stacked contractive autoencoders can be effectively used as an unsupervised approach to preprocess infrared microscopic pixel spectra.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same unsupervised preprocessing claim with equivalent wording and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The resulting neural networks can reliably resolve tissue structure after supervised fine-tuning.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The resulting neural networks can reliably resolve tissue structure after supervised fine-tuning.
alignments.rationale: The supervised fine-tuning result is preserved without meaningful change in scope or confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A network trained on embedded tissue can be successfully transferred to classify fresh frozen tissue.
alignments.pub_claim_text: A network trained on embedded tissue can be successfully transferred to classify fresh frozen tissue.
alignments.rationale: The publication presents the transfer result but with reduced stated certainty (medium vs high), so the semantic claim is unchanged but confidence is tempered.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The features obtained from unsupervised pretraining generalize across the large spectral differences between embedded and fresh frozen tissue.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The features obtained from unsupervised pretraining generalize across the large spectral differences between embedded and fresh frozen tissue.
alignments.rationale: The generalization claim is restated identically with the same level of certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Switch costs emerged only when targets were imposed and not when target selection was free.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Switch costs emerged only when targets were imposed and not when target selection was free.
alignments.rationale: The behavioral result about switch costs is reproduced verbatim between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Differential levels of activity in the frontoparietal control network were found depending on whether target switches were free or imposed.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Differential levels of activity in the frontoparietal control network were found depending on whether target switches were free or imposed.
alignments.rationale: The neural-activity finding in frontoparietal control regions is unchanged in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Core regions of the default mode network were active during target repetitions, indicating reduced control on these trials.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Core regions of the default mode network were active during target repetitions, indicating reduced control on these trials.
alignments.rationale: The DMN activation result for repetitions is preserved exactly.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Free and imposed switches jointly activated parietal and posterior frontal cortices, while free switches additionally activated anterior frontal cortices.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Free and imposed switches jointly activated parietal and posterior frontal cortices, while free switches additionally activated anterior frontal cortices.
alignments.rationale: The activation pattern claim is the same but the publication presents it with higher certainty, amplifying confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The findings highlight unique contributions of proactive and reactive control during visual search.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The findings highlight unique contributions of proactive and reactive control during visual search.
alignments.rationale: The interpretive conclusion is preserved but the publication reports it with slightly lower stated certainty than the preprint, so confidence is tempered.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The derived approximation to gradient-based learning is consistent with biologically motivated constraints such as causality and locality. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The new learning rule derived for RNNs requires synaptic weight updates to depend only on local information about pre- and postsynaptic activities. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication reframes the preprint's claim from a general consistency with constraints to a stronger statement that updates must depend only on local pre/post information, which narrows the original scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The online learning rule for synaptic weights utilizes only local information about pre- and postsynaptic activities along with a random feedback projection of the RNN output error. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The new learning rule derived for RNNs requires synaptic weight updates to depend only on local information about pre- and postsynaptic activities. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication keeps the core locality claim but omits the random feedback/output-error detail from the preprint, clarifying and narrowing the stated mechanism.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Simulations demonstrate that the new learning rule can effectively train an RNN to perform a variety of tasks. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Simulations demonstrate that the new learning rule can be used to train an RNN to perform a variety of tasks. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the simulation result with effectively identical wording and scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: An augmented circuit architecture is proposed to enable the RNN to concatenate short-duration patterns into sequences of longer duration. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: An augmented circuit architecture is proposed to allow the RNN to concatenate short-duration patterns into longer sequences. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the proposal and intended function of the augmented architecture with equivalent scope and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Repeatable and reliable patterns of spatiotemporal activity can be generated in chaotic and noisy spiking recurrent neural networks. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Repeatable and reliable patterns of spatiotemporal activity can be generated in chaotic and noisy spiking recurrent neural networks. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the preprint result identically.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model accurately learns a variety of tasks, including speech generation, motor control and spatial navigation. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The model accurately learns a variety of tasks, including speech generation, motor control, and spatial navigation. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The claim is carried over verbatim with no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model performs temporal rescaling of natural spoken words and exhibits sequential neural activity commonly found in experimental data involving temporal processing. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The model performs temporal rescaling of natural spoken words and exhibits sequential neural activity commonly found in experimental data involving temporal processing. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same empirical result without change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Combining oscillatory neuronal inputs with different frequencies provides a key mechanism to generate precisely timed sequences of activity in recurrent circuits of the brain. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Combining oscillatory neuronal inputs with different frequencies provides a key mechanism to generate precisely timed sequences of activity in recurrent circuits of the brain. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The interpretive conclusion is preserved exactly in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Convolutional neural networks (CNNs) trained on mixed Poisson and Gaussian noise images can overcome the limitations of existing image denoising methods. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Convolutional neural networks (CNNs) trained on mixed Poisson and Gaussian noise images can overcome the limitations of existing image denoising methods. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the same high-level conclusion about CNNs trained on mixed-noise images.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The trained CNN performs real-time image denoising within tens of milliseconds with superior performance compared to conventional fluorescence microscopy denoising methods. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The trained CNN performs real-time image denoising within tens of milliseconds with superior performance compared to conventional fluorescence microscopy denoising methods. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: This empirical performance claim is carried over verbatim to the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The method consistently achieves high performance (> 8 dB) denoising in less time than other fluorescence microscopy denoising methods. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The method consistently achieves high-performance denoising on external datasets with out-of-distribution noise, contrast, structure, and imaging modalities. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication replaces the specific >8 dB/time comparison with a broader claim about generalization to external, out-of-distribution datasets, expanding scope but changing the metric and emphasis.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A CNN can be trained to correct the errors of the Random Forest in the source domain and then be applied to correct such errors in the target domain without retraining. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A CNN can be trained to correct the errors of the Random Forest in the source domain and then be applied to correct such errors in the target domain without retraining. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this methodological/result claim verbatim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The method can deliver segmentations that are sufficiently accurate to act as pseudo-labels for target-domain CNN training. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The method can deliver segmentations that are sufficiently accurate to act as pseudo-labels for target-domain CNN training. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: This performance-as-pseudo-labels claim is unchanged in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The performance of the method yields excellent results compared to microscopy domain adaptation baselines, especially when a significant domain shift is involved. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The performance of the method on several datasets yields excellent performance compared to microscopy domain adaptation baselines. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication separates the general excellent-performance claim (Q2) from the condition about large domain shifts (Q3), clarifying rather than changing the original assertion.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: An asynchronous regime or periodic oscillations spontaneously emerge in a balanced network of identical QIF neurons depending on parameter values. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: An asynchronous regime or periodic oscillations spontaneously emerge depending on the parameter values in a balanced network of identical quadratic integrate-and-fire neurons. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the result with the expanded form of the QIF acronym but preserves meaning and scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The mean field model based on a self-consistent Fokker-Planck equation reproduces the asynchronous dynamics in the homogeneous case. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The mean-field model based on a self-consistent Fokker-Planck equation reproduces the asynchronous dynamics in the homogeneous case. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The claim is preserved verbatim in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: An exact self-consistent solution for the mean firing rate allows identifying balanced regimes that can be either mean- or fluctuation-driven. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: An exact self-consistent solution for the mean firing rate in the limit of infinite in-degree identifies balanced regimes that can be either mean- or fluctuation-driven. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication adds the technical condition ('in the limit of infinite in-degree'), narrowing and clarifying the result while presenting it with higher certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Two cumulants suffice to reproduce the transition scenario observed in the network. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Two cumulants suffice to reproduce the transition scenario observed in the network. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the two-cumulant finding with essentially the same claim and scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The emergence of periodic collective oscillations is well captured by the mean field models upon tuning either the connectivity or the input DC current. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The emergence of periodic collective oscillations is well captured by the mean-field models upon tuning either the connectivity or the input DC current. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: This result is retained in the publication without substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The classification methods performed equally in terms of sensitivity and specificity. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The five classification methods performed equally in terms of sensitivity and specificity. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same result with equivalent wording and certainty about equal sensitivity and specificity across the five methods.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The area under the ROC was significantly better for Adaptive Boosting, Logistic Regression, Artificial Neural Networks, and Random Forest. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Adaptive boosting, logistic regression, artificial neural networks, and random forest demonstrated significantly better results for AUC<sub>roc</sub> and the Dice coefficient compared to the other methods. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication makes the same AUCroc finding but adds results for the Dice coefficient, broadening the set of reported performance metrics while keeping the claim's confidence high.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: There was no statistically significant difference between the performances of the five classification methods regarding the area under the precision-recall curve. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: There was no statistically significant difference between the performances of the five classification methods regarding AUC<sub>pr</sub>. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same null result for AUCpr with equivalent certainty and wording.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed network model can encode and reproduce temporal aspects of the input. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed network model is able to encode and reproduce temporal aspects of the input. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the claim verbatim about encoding and reproducing temporal aspects with the same certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The relationship between the structure of the weight matrix, the dynamical network parameters, and the temporal aspects of sequence recall is analytically characterized. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: An analytical characterization of the relationship between the structure of the weight matrix, the dynamical network parameters, and the temporal aspects of sequence recall is provided. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the analytic characterization but reports it with reduced stated certainty (medium), so the content is unchanged while confidence is tempered.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The inclusion of modularity in the network structure facilitates the learning and recall of multiple overlapping sequences even in a noisy regime. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The inclusion of modularity in the network structure facilitates the learning and recall of multiple overlapping sequences even in a noisy regime. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication presents the same result about modularity facilitating learning/recall under noise with equivalent wording and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Distinct representations of multiple environments can coexist in grid cells. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Distinct representations of multiple environments can coexist in grid cells. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this key result verbatim with the same level of certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Existing models for grid cells have the potential to express several sets of hexagonal grid patterns. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Existing models for grid cells have the potential to express several sets of hexagonal grid patterns. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves this conclusion with equivalent wording and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A population of grid cells can encode multiple non-congruent metric relationships. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A population of grid cells can encode multiple noncongruent metric relationships. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication conveys the same conclusion (minor orthographic difference only) with the same certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Grid-like codes may represent environments with a variety of different geometries and possibly conceptual and cognitive spaces. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Grid-like codes may represent environments with a variety of different geometries and cognitive spaces. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication removes the hedging word 'possibly' and omits explicit mention of 'conceptual' spaces, making the claim somewhat more specific (narrower) while keeping the same confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed method achieves cross modality image matching by jointly estimating polynomial transformations of the atlas intensity along with pose and deformation parameters. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The registration method allows for cross-modality image matching by jointly estimating polynomial transformations of the atlas intensity. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication affirms joint estimation of polynomial intensity transforms for cross-modality matching but omits explicit mention of pose and deformation in the concise claim, narrowing the original methodological detail.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Missing data is effectively accommodated through a multiple atlas selection procedure that utilizes several atlases of homogeneous intensity. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed method for diffeomorphic image registration effectively accommodates missing data through a multiple atlas selection procedure. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication conveys the same result about handling missing data via multiple-atlas selection within the registration framework with equivalent certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The combination of the two new concepts within an Expectation Maximization algorithm allows for iterative updates of atlas selection posteriors and deformation parameters. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed method for diffeomorphic image registration effectively accommodates missing data through a multiple atlas selection procedure. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication emphasizes the multiple-atlas selection outcome but does not explicitly state the EM-based iterative-update mechanism in the summary claim, so the detailed algorithmic phrasing is less explicit though the procedural result is retained.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The method enables the quantification of the 3D density distribution of tauopathy in the medial temporal lobe of an Alzheimer's disease postmortem specimen. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: This registration method enables the quantification of the 3D density distribution of tauopathy in Alzheimer's disease postmortem specimens. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the quantification capability and broadens the wording from a single specimen to specimens (plural), slightly broadening the scope while keeping the claim otherwise unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Child abuse was associated with increased resting-state functional connectivity between various theory-of-mind regions including the precuneus and the brainstem. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Child abuse was associated with increased resting-state functional connectivity between various theory-of-mind regions, suggesting altered hierarchical processing. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication keeps the core association but omits the specific region list and adds an interpretation about altered hierarchical processing, shifting emphasis and slightly narrowing anatomical specificity.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Resting-state functional connectivity was also changed between the dorsomedial prefrontal cortex and the cerebellum. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: No publication claim corresponds to the specific dmPFC–cerebellum connectivity finding; the publication instead reports different region-specific effects, so this specific preprint claim does not appear in the set of publication summary claims.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The data indicate a lasting influence of early life stress on neural networks involved in social processing. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The data indicate a lasting impact of childhood maltreatment on the neural networks involved in social information processing. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the same conclusion about lasting impact on social-processing networks but expresses it with higher stated certainty (from medium to high), thus amplifying confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Altered hierarchical processing in theory-of-mind regions may underlie the social difficulties reported by maltreated individuals. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Altered neural networks may account for some of the interpersonal difficulties victims of childhood maltreatment experience. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication generalizes the explanatory claim from altered hierarchical processing in ToM regions to altered neural networks more broadly as an account for interpersonal difficulties, broadening the mechanistic scope while retaining similar confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: In the mouse medial prefrontal cortex (mPFC), neural activity during the first two postnatal weeks decorrelates following specific spatial patterns. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: In the mouse medial prefrontal cortex (mPFC), neural activity during the first two postnatal weeks decorrelates following specific spatial patterns. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Text is essentially identical between preprint and publication, indicating the result was carried forward unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A relative increase of inhibition drives the decorrelation of neural activity. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A relative increase of inhibition drives the decorrelation of neural activity. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The claim is reproduced verbatim in the publication with the same certainty and meaning.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Subtle alterations in the excitation/inhibition (E-I) ratio are associated with specific impairments in the correlational structure of spike trains in two mouse models of neurodevelopmental disorders. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Subtle alterations in the excitation-inhibition (E-I) ratio are associated with specific impairments in the correlational structure of spike trains in mice mimicking the etiology of neurodevelopmental disorders. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the same core finding but slightly rephrases the description of the mouse models, clarifying their relation to etiology; overall meaning and scope are preserved.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: An analogous developmental transition takes place in the human brain, as shown by EEG data from newborn babies. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: An analogous developmental transition takes place in the human brain, as shown by EEG data from newborn babies. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The human EEG result is restated identically in the publication with unchanged certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Machine learning algorithms increase the confidence of markers initially identified by GWAS and uncover new candidate regions that could serve as associated markers.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The top-ranked markers from SVM and RF aligned with GWAS findings for solid stem pith, winter standability, and winter injury.
alignments.rationale: The publication reports alignment between ML top-ranked markers and GWAS for specific traits but does not support the broader preprint claim that ML generally increases confidence or uncovers new candidate regions, so the claim is narrowed and presented less strongly.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The identified markers can facilitate marker-assisted selection to expedite breeding programs to increase winter hardiness or stem palatability.
alignments.pub_claim_text: These markers can facilitate marker-assisted selection to expedite breeding programs to increase winter hardiness or stem palatability.
alignments.rationale: The publication reiterates the applied utility of the markers for marker-assisted selection and breeding with the same framing and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Genes harboring markers associated to the four stem traits play roles in plant growth, response to plant injury, or tolerance to cold.
alignments.rationale: The publication's listed claims do not include statements about the functional roles of genes harboring the markers, so this specific biological-interpretation claim is not present in the publication claims.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Proline (P) and glutamine (Q) are highly abundant in celiac disease associated peptides.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Proline (P) and glutamine (Q) are highly abundant in celiac disease associated peptides.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates this compositional observation identically.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A model based on the density of P&Q in peptides has been developed for predicting celiac disease associated peptides, achieving a maximum AUROC of 0.98.
alignments.pub_claim_text: A model based on the density of P and Q in peptides has been developed for predicting celiac disease associated peptides, achieving a maximum AUROC of 0.98 on independent data.
alignments.rationale: The publication adds that the reported AUROC was achieved on independent data, which clarifies the evaluation but does not change the core performance claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The ensemble model predicts celiac disease associated motifs with 100% accuracy on an independent dataset.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The ensemble model predicts celiac disease associated motifs with 100% accuracy on an independent dataset not used for training.
alignments.rationale: The publication specifies that the independent dataset was not used for training, which clarifies the original claim while preserving its substance and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A web server and standalone software package named 'CDpred' has been developed for predicting celiac disease associated peptides and motifs.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The best models and motifs have been integrated into a web server and standalone software package called 'CDpred' for the prediction and design of celiac disease associated peptides.
alignments.rationale: The publication keeps the same contribution (CDpred web server and standalone package) but presents it with slightly lower certainty, so the claim content is unchanged while confidence is tempered.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: IL-13 plays an important role in increasing the severity of COVID-19 and other diseases. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication does not state that IL-13 increases disease severity; it only analyzes IL-13 induction by variants, so the explicit severity claim is absent.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: IL-13Pred is a highly accurate in-silico method developed for predicting IL-13 inducing peptides/epitopes. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The best model based on XGBoost achieves a maximum AUC of 0.83 and 0.80 on the training and independent dataset, respectively. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication provides explicit performance metrics (AUCs) for the model, which clarifies and strengthens the preprint's assertion of high accuracy.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: IL-13 inducing peptides are reported in various SARS-CoV2 strains/variants proteins. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our analysis indicates that certain SARS-COV2 variants are more prone to induce IL-13 in COVID-19 patients. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication shifts from a general report of peptides across strains to a more specific finding that certain variants are particularly prone to IL-13 induction, narrowing and clarifying the original claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The developed method can be used to detect IL-13 inducing peptides in vaccine candidates. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The best performing model was incorporated in a web-server and standalone package named 'IL-13Pred' for precise prediction of IL-13 inducing peptides. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication documents deployment of the model for prediction of IL-13-inducing peptides but does not explicitly claim application to vaccine candidates, so the application claim is less explicit.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A user-friendly web server and standalone software for IL-13Pred is freely available. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The best performing model was incorporated in a web-server and standalone package named 'IL-13Pred' for precise prediction of IL-13 inducing peptides. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication explicitly reports the web-server and standalone package named IL-13Pred, directly matching the preprint availability claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Anaerobic Myxococcota possess smaller genomes and fewer genes encoding biosynthetic gene clusters, peptidases, signal transduction systems, and transcriptional regulators compared to their soil counterparts. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Anaerobic Myxococcota possess smaller genomes and fewer genes encoding biosynthetic gene clusters, peptidases, signal transduction systems, and transcriptional regulators compared to their soil counterparts. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same result verbatim, so there is no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The genomes of Zodletone Myxococcota lack aerobic respiratory capacities but encode genes suggestive of fermentation, dissimilatory nitrite reduction, and dissimilatory sulfate-reduction for energy acquisition. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The genomes of Zodletone Myxococcota lack aerobic respiratory capacities but contain genes suggestive of fermentation, dissimilatory nitrite reduction, and dissimilatory sulfate-reduction for energy acquisition. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication's claim is essentially identical in content, indicating no change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The lack of characteristic hallmarks for social behavior in the genomes of these Myxococcota suggests a non-social behavior. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Machine learning approaches predicted a nonsocial behavior for Zodletone Myxococcota based on a set of 634 genes informative of social lifestyle. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication provides a specific ML-based prediction supporting the preprint's suggestion of nonsocial behavior, clarifying and strengthening the original inference with a concrete method and gene set.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Predation and cellular differentiation in Myxococcota represent a niche adaptation strategy that evolved circa 500 million years ago in response to the rise of soil as a distinct habitat. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Predation and cellular differentiation in Myxococcota represent a niche adaptation strategy that evolved circa 500 million years ago in response to the rise of soil as a distinct habitat on Earth. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the evolutionary timing and ecological interpretation with only a minor wording addition ('on Earth'), so the claim is effectively unchanged.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The perceived lack of social capacity in the genomes of the novel Myxococcota was confirmed through detailed comparative genomic analysis of 13 pathways associated with Myxococcota social behavior. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: This publication claim (detailed comparative genomic confirmation) was not present in the preprint and represents an added, stronger result confirming the lack of social capacity.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The breakdown of frontoparietal alpha networks and the emergence of frontoparietal theta connectivity occur as participants stop responding to stimuli during drowsiness. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The breakdown of alpha networks and the emergence of theta connectivity occur as participants become drowsy and unresponsive. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the finding with equivalent meaning (drowsy/unresponsive), so the claim is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The temporal dynamics of all canonical EEG microstates slow down during unresponsiveness. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The temporal dynamics of all canonical EEG microstates slow down during unresponsiveness. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Identical claim in publication; no change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Microstate D's occurrence and duration are significantly increased during the period of unresponsiveness. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Microstate D's occurrence and duration are prominently increased during the period of unresponsiveness. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication makes the same result claim (language slightly different but equivalent).

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The temporal properties of microstate D, particularly its prolonged duration, predict the response likelihood to individual stimuli. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The temporal properties of microstate D, particularly its prolonged duration, predict the response likelihood to individual stimuli. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the predictive relationship exactly; no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The transition to unconsciousness consists of an interplay between transient brain networks reflecting different degrees of sleep depth. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The transition to unconsciousness consists of an interplay between transient brain networks reflecting different degrees of sleep depth. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Conclusion is restated unchanged in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: CausCell outperforms the state-of-the-art methods in both disentanglement and reconstruction scenarios. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: CausCell outperforms the state-of-the-art methods in both disentanglement and reconstruction scenarios. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the performance claim exactly; no change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: CausCell increases the explainability, generalizability, and controllability of single-cell data compared to existing black-box representation learning models. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: CausCell increases the explainability, generalizability, and controllability of single-cell data compared to existing black-box representation learning models. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The conceptual claim is carried forward unchanged into the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: CausCell can implement controllable generation by intervening with the concepts of single-cell data when given a causal structure. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: CausCell can implement controllable generation by intervening with the concepts of single-cell data when given a causal structure. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication presents the same capability claim without substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: CausCell has the potential to uncover biological insights by generating counterfactuals from small and noisy single-cell datasets. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: CausCell has the potential to uncover biological insights by generating counterfactuals from small and noisy single-cell datasets. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The substance of the claim is the same; only the framing/type label changed from contribution to conclusion in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The combination of non-linearities in the stochastic reaction-diffusion model can lead prion propagation to behave as a complex system. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The combination of non-linearities in the stochastic reaction-diffusion model can lead prion propagation to behave as a complex system. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same modeling result verbatim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This model provides an alternative to the current paradigm to explain strain specific phenotypes, tissue tropisms, and strain co-propagation. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: This model provides an alternative to the current paradigm to explain strain-specific phenotypes and tissue tropisms. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication omits 'strain co-propagation' and downgrades certainty to medium, so the claim is narrowed and presented with more tempered confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The research clarifies the role of the connectome in the neuro-invasion process of prion diseases. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The model clarifies the role of the connectome in the neuro-invasion process. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication carries forward the same conclusion about the connectome's role, with only minor wording differences.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Bilateral dLMCs were engaged with effector specificity, and this engagement was left-lateralized with right upregulation in noise. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Bilateral dLMCs were engaged with effector specificity, and this engagement was left-lateralized with right upregulation in noise. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The preprint and publication text are effectively identical in content and certainty, so the claim is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The dLMC contributed to various decision stages depending on the hemisphere and task difficulty. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The dLMC contributed to various decision stages depending on the hemisphere and task difficulty. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Claim wording and certainty match between preprint and publication, indicating no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: These findings substantially advance our understanding of the hemispherical lateralization and temporal dynamics of bilateral dLMC in sensorimotor integration during speech perceptual decision-making. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: These findings substantially advance our understanding of the hemispherical lateralization and temporal dynamics of bilateral dLMC in sensorimotor integration during speech perceptual decision-making. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The conclusion is reproduced verbatim in the publication with the same certainty, so it is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: DeepAllo is the first study that combines a fine-tuned protein language model with FPocket features to predict allosteric sites. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: DeepAllo is the first study that combines a fine-tuned protein language model with FPocket features to predict allosteric sites. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The contribution claim is repeated exactly in the publication, indicating no change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The best model developed in this study predicts allosteric pockets with an F1 score of 89.66% and identifies 90.5% of allosteric pockets in the top 3 positions. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The best model predicts allosteric pockets with an 89.66% F1 score and 90.5% of allosteric pockets in the top 3 positions, outperforming previous results. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The numeric performance is the same but the publication adds a comparative statement (outperforming previous results), which clarifies and amplifies the claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The approach has been validated through a case study on proteins with known allosteric pockets. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A case study on proteins with known allosteric pockets demonstrates the effectiveness of the proposed approach. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The case-study result is present in both versions but the publication reduces the stated certainty (high->medium), so the claim's confidence is tempered.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study includes an effort to explain the protein language model by visualizing its attention mechanism among allosteric and non-allosteric residues. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication does not report the attention-visualization explanation effort present in the preprint, indicating the claim was removed.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The pLM model was fine-tuned on the AlloSteric Database in a Multitask Learning setting and used as a feature extractor for training other models. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: This methodological detail about fine-tuning and use as a feature extractor is present in the publication but was not in the preprint, so it is an added claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: See-Star is a hydrogel-based tissue clearing protocol that renders opaque and calcified invertebrates optically transparent while preserving their anatomy. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: See-Star is a hydrogel-based tissue clearing protocol that renders opaque and calcified invertebrates optically transparent while preserving their anatomy. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The core contribution statement is identical between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The See-Star protocol can clear large specimens greater than 1 cm³ to enable deep-tissue imaging. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The See-Star protocol can clear large specimens greater than 1 cm3 to enable deep-tissue imaging. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The result about clearing large specimens is reproduced without substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: See-Star is compatible with molecular techniques such as immunohistochemistry and in situ hybridization for visualizing protein and mRNA localization. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: See-Star is compatible with molecular techniques such as immunohistochemistry and in situ hybridization for visualizing protein and mRNA localization. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Compatibility with molecular techniques is stated the same way in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The method allows for comparative studies of intact nervous systems in juvenile echinoderms and molluscs, facilitating insights into their anatomy. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The method allows for comparative studies of intact nervous systems in juvenile echinoderms and molluscs, extending insights into their anatomy. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication reframes the claim more cautiously (certainty reduced to medium) and slightly shifts wording to 'extending insights,' so it is a clarified claim with tempered confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: EVEscape quantifies the viral escape potential of mutations at scale and is applicable before surveillance sequencing is available. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: EVEscape quantifies the viral escape potential of mutations at scale and is applicable before surveillance sequencing is available. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The contribution is restated identically in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: EVEscape, trained on sequences available prior to 2020, is as accurate as high-throughput experimental scans at anticipating pandemic variation for SARS-CoV-2. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: EVEscape, trained on sequences before 2020, is as accurate as high-throughput experimental scans at anticipating pandemic variation for SARS-CoV-2. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The result and training-time framing remain the same and are reproduced in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: EVEscape is generalizable to other viruses including Influenza, HIV, and understudied viruses with pandemic potential such as Lassa and Nipah. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: EVEscape is generalizable to other viruses including influenza, HIV, and understudied viruses with pandemic potential such as Lassa and Nipah. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The generalizability claim is reproduced with consistent scope and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: EVEscape provides continually updated escape scores for all current strains of SARS-CoV-2 and predicts likely additional mutations to forecast emerging strains. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: EVEscape provides continually revised escape scores for all current strains of SARS-CoV-2 and predicts probable further mutations to forecast emerging strains. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Minor wording changes (updated->revised, likely->probable) do not alter the substantive claim, so it is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: SmartScope is the first framework to streamline, standardize, and automate specimen evaluation in cryo-electron microscopy. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: SmartScope is the first framework to streamline, standardize, and automate specimen evaluation in cryo-electron microscopy. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The core claim of being the first framework is unchanged between versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: SmartScope employs deep-learning-based object detection to identify and classify features suitable for imaging. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: SmartScope employs deep-learning-based object detection to identify and classify features suitable for imaging. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The methodological/result statement is the same in both documents.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The automated tool for systematic evaluation of specimens streamlines structure determination and lowers the barrier of adoption for cryo-electron microscopy. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The automated tool for systematic evaluation of specimens streamlines structure determination and lowers the barrier of adoption for cryo-EM. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The conclusion is reproduced with equivalent meaning (cryo-electron microscopy abbreviated), indicating no substantive change.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Manual annotations can be used to re-train the feature recognition models, leading to improvements in performance. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: This new procedural/result claim about retraining with manual annotations does not appear in the preprint and is therefore an added publication claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: CIGER significantly outperforms existing methods in both ranking and classification metrics for predicting gene expression profiles induced by de novo chemicals. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The chemical-induced gene expression ranking (CIGER) framework significantly outperforms existing methods in both ranking and classification metrics. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same performance claim with equivalent emphasis and metrics; wording is effectively identical.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A new drug screening pipeline based on CIGER is proposed to select approved or investigational drugs for potential treatments of pancreatic cancer. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A drug screening pipeline based on CIGER is proposed to identify potential treatments for drug-resistant pancreatic cancer. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication refines the pipeline's target to drug-resistant pancreatic cancer (more specific), so the claim is clarified and its scope narrowed, with similar confidence level.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The predictions made by CIGER have been validated by experiments, demonstrating its effectiveness for phenotypic compound screening in precision drug discovery. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The predictions made by CIGER have been validated by experiments, demonstrating its effectiveness for phenotypic compound screening in precision medicine. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The experimental validation claim remains but the application wording shifts from 'precision drug discovery' to the broader 'precision medicine', a clarification/broadening of context without change in asserted confidence.

alignments.pub_claim_text: CIGER targets a more realistic but challenging setting for predicting overall rankings in gene expression profiles induced by de novo chemicals. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: This contribution about targeting a more realistic/challenging prediction setting is present in the publication but was not stated in the preprint; it appears as an added clarification of the task.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The A3D Database provides structure-based aggregation predictions for over 17 thousand non-membrane human proteins. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The A3D database provides structure-based aggregation predictions for the human proteome using the Aggrescan3D tool. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication frames the resource as providing proteome-wide predictions (broader) and names the tool used; this is a clarification/broadening of the original numeric/non-membrane phrasing.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The A3D Database includes graphical tools for visualizing and interpreting protein structure datasets. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The A3D database includes graphical tools for visualizing and interpreting protein structure datasets. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The graphical tools claim is restated verbatim in the publication with equivalent meaning.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The database allows for testing the influence of user-selected mutations on protein solubility and stability. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The A3D database allows users to test the influence of selected mutations on protein solubility and stability. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The A3D Database can be used to analyze physiologically relevant proteins through case studies. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The A3D database analyzes over 20.5 thousand unique Uniprot IDs in terms of their aggregation properties. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication adds a specific quantitative result (20.5k Uniprot IDs) that was not present in the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: AlphaFold2 predicts the holo form of a protein in 70% of the cases. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: AlphaFold2 predicts the holo form of a protein in approximately 70% of the cases. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: AlphaFold2 is unable to reproduce the observed conformational diversity with an equivalent error than in the estimation of a single conformation. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: AlphaFold2 is unable to reproduce the observed conformational diversity with the same error for both conformers. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: AlphaFold2’s performance worsens with the increasing conformational diversity of the studied protein. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: AlphaFold2's performance worsens with the increasing conformational diversity of the studied protein. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The performance degradation with conformational diversity is expressed identically in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The impairment in AlphaFold2's performance is related to the heterogeneity in the degree of conformational diversity found between different members of the homologous family of the protein under study. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Main-chain flexibility associated with apo-holo pairs of conformers negatively correlates with the predicted local model quality score plDDT. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Main-chain flexibility associated with apo-holo pairs of conformers negatively correlates with the predicted local model quality score plDDT. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The correlation between main-chain flexibility and plDDT is stated the same way in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We present an approach to evaluating cell segmentation methods without relying upon comparison to results from humans. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: We present an approach to evaluating cell segmentation methods without relying on human comparison. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We defined a number of segmentation quality metrics that can be applied to multichannel fluorescence images. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: We defined a number of segmentation quality metrics that can be applied to multichannel fluorescence images. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The metric-definition claim is unchanged between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Two deep learning-based methods performed the best overall in our evaluation of segmentation methods. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Two deep learning-based methods performed the best overall in our evaluation of segmentation methods. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: Results for all segmentation methods could be significantly improved by postprocessing to ensure proper matching of cell and nuclear masks. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our evaluation tool is available as open source and all code and data are available in a Reproducible Research Archive. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our evaluation tool is available as open source and all code and data are available in a Reproducible Research Archive. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A molecular cross-docking-based approach was developed for the identification of protein partners. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The molecular cross-docking-based approach significantly improves the identification of protein partners compared to previous methods. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The approach achieved significant improvement compared to previous works. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The molecular cross-docking-based approach significantly improves the identification of protein partners compared to previous methods. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: This performance-improvement claim is stated equivalently in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The method demonstrated very high discriminative power on specific functional classes. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The docking algorithm demonstrates very high discriminative power on specific functional classes of proteins. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The discriminative-power result is preserved verbatim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The approach was assessed for its ability to account for protein surface multiple usages. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The approach is capable of accounting for protein surface multiple usages. (certainty=medium, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The study provides insights into the contributions of shape and physico-chemical complementarity, interface matching, and specificity in protein interaction predictions. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The developed approach demonstrates the state-of-the-art performance on several binding affinity benchmarking datasets. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The attention-based pooling of protein graphs enables identification of surface residues as critical residues for protein-ligand binding. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The attention-based pooling of protein graphs enables identification of surface residues as critical residues for protein-ligand binding. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same claim verbatim about attention-based pooling identifying critical surface residues.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We validate our model predictions against an experimental assay on a viral main protease (Mpro)– the hallmark target of SARS-CoV-2 coronavirus. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The model predictions are validated against an experimental assay on a viral main protease (Mpro) target of SARS-CoV-2. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Same validation claim with equivalent wording and confidence; no substantive change in scope or certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The GANscan method allows for image acquisitions at 30 times the throughput of traditional stop-and-stare systems. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The GANscan method allows for image acquisitions at 30 times the throughput of traditional stop-and-stare systems. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the exact throughput claim with the same certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: GANscan successfully restores sharp images from motion blurred videos during high-speed scanning. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: GANscan successfully restores sharp images from motion blurred videos during high-speed scanning. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Identical claim about restoration of sharp images from motion blur; no change in scope or confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed method corrects defocusing that occurs during scanning. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The method effectively adjusts for defocusing that occurs during scanning within +/− 5 μm. (certainty=medium, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The inference of the GANscan method runs at less than 20 milliseconds per image using a consumer GPU. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The runtime claim is reproduced exactly with the same performance bound and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We have developed an automated microscopy pipeline coupled with deep learning-based image analysis for the high-throughput phenotyping of plant-pathogen interactions. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: BluVision Micro is a modular, machine learning-aided system designed for high-throughput microscopic phenotyping. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same contribution (automated/ML-aided high-throughput microscopy) with similar emphasis on system capabilities.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The system can quantify fungal microcolony count and density, the precise area of the secondary hyphae of each colony, and different morphological parameters. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: BluVision Micro was applied to screen 196 genetically diverse barley genotypes for interactions with powdery mildew fungi, delivering accurate, sensitive, and reproducible results. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication frames the capabilities in the context of a specific large screen (196 genotypes) and reports accuracy/reproducibility, narrowing the original broad measurement claim to an applied validation.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our tool opens the nonhost resistance phenomenon to genetics and genomics studies. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The application of BluVision Micro enabled the identification of novel genetic loci and marker-trait associations in the barley genome. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The preprint's more speculative claim about enabling genetics/genomics studies is made concrete in the publication by reporting identified loci, which amplifies confidence and broadens from a methodological opening to demonstrated discovery.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We have validated the system’s performance in disease resistance screens of genetically diverse barley material. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: BluVision Micro was applied to screen 196 genetically diverse barley genotypes for interactions with powdery mildew fungi, delivering accurate, sensitive, and reproducible results. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication provides the concrete validation context (196 genotypes) and performance descriptors, clarifying the preprint's validation claim.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The application of BluVision Micro enabled the identification of novel genetic loci and marker-trait associations in the barley genome. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: BluVision's open-source software supports the development of specific modules for various microscopic phenotypes. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The fully automated deep learning based markerless motion capture workflow demonstrated high levels of agreement for lower limb joint angles compared to marker-based motion capture. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The fully automated deep learning based markerless motion capture workflow demonstrated high levels of agreement for lower limb joint angles compared to marker-based motion capture. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Mean differences in lower limb joint angles ranged between 0.1° - 10.5° for 6 DoF hip joint rotations, and 0.7° - 3.9° for knee and ankle rotations. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Mean differences in joint rotations for the markerless approach ranged from 0.1° to 10.5° for hip joint rotations and 0.7° to 3.9° for knee and ankle rotations. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Numeric ranges and joints reported are identical between preprint and publication, so the claim is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The differences in joint angles generally fall within the documented uncertainties of marker-based motion capture, suggesting the markerless approach could be used for appropriate biomechanics applications. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The differences in joint angles generally fall within the documented uncertainties of marker-based motion capture, suggesting the markerless approach is suitable for biomechanics applications. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
