alignments.preprint_claim_text: The open-source, modular and customisable workflow allows for integration with other popular biomechanics tools such as OpenSim. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The open-source, modular and customizable workflow allows for integration with other popular biomechanics tools such as OpenSim. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this contribution verbatim with the same emphasis.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The light-VGG11 model was developed to automatically identify chicken distress calls with a significantly smaller size in parameters and faster detection speed compared to the original VGG11. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The light-VGG11 model achieved precision of 94.58%, recall of 94.89%, F1-score of 94.73%, and accuracy of 95.07% in identifying distress calls. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The light-VGG11 model demonstrates comparable performance to VGG11 with significantly fewer parameters and faster detection speed. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: Data augmentation techniques can improve distress call detection by up to 1.52%. (certainty=medium, type=result)

alignments.rationale: This result about predicting new domain arrangements on CASP15 targets does not appear in the preprint claims and is therefore an added publication claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: DeepGCF demonstrated a better prediction performance compared to the previous functional conservation prediction method.
alignments.pub_claim_text: DeepGCF demonstrated better prediction performance compared with the previous method.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same performance claim with nearly identical wording and emphasis; no substantive change in meaning.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The resulting DeepGCF score captures the functional conservation by examining chromatin states, sequence ontologies, and regulatory variants.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The resulting DeepGCF score captures the functional conservation between humans and pigs by examining chromatin states, sequence ontologies, and regulatory variants.
alignments.rationale: The publication adds a species-specific scope (humans and pigs), which clarifies and narrows the original, more general claim while keeping the core mechanism the same.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Regions with higher DeepGCF score play a more important role in regulatory activities and show heritability enrichment in human complex traits and diseases.
alignments.pub_claim_text: We identified a core set of genomic regions as functionally conserved that plays key roles in gene regulation and is enriched for the heritability of complex traits and diseases in humans.
alignments.rationale: The publication frames the observation as a defined "core set" of regions (more specific) but conveys the same finding of regulatory importance and heritability enrichment.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our DeepGCF approach shows a promising application on the comparison of cross-species functional conservation.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our results highlight the importance of cross-species functional comparison in illustrating the genetic and evolutionary basis of complex phenotypes.
alignments.rationale: The publication strengthens and broadens the take-home message, linking cross-species comparison to genetic/evolutionary insights for complex phenotypes, which amplifies the original conclusion.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The functional conservation information will provide additional insight into the genetic and evolutionary mechanisms behind complex traits and diseases.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our results highlight the importance of cross-species functional comparison in illustrating the genetic and evolutionary basis of complex phenotypes.
alignments.rationale: This preprint conclusion overlaps substantially with the publication's emphasis on evolutionary/genetic insights from cross-species comparison; the publication slightly reframes but does not contradict it.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We demonstrate an integrated method to elucidate the structural ensemble of the NCBD/ACTR complex using selective labeling SANS experiments, molecular dynamics simulations, and a deep learning algorithm.
alignments.pub_claim_text: We demonstrate an integrated method to elucidate the structural ensemble of a complex formed by two IDRs.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the integrated-method claim but generalizes the specific NCBD/ACTR example to a more general two-IDR complex, clarifying scope toward broader applicability.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The structural ensemble we describe captures the experimental SANS and NMR data better than MD ensembles generated by one single force field.
alignments.pub_claim_text: We present an ensemble that describes experimental SANS and NMR data better than MD ensembles generated by one single force field.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same result claim about the presented ensemble outperforming single-force-field MD ensembles with effectively identical meaning.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Deep learning reveals three clusters of distinct conformations in the structural ensemble based on structural similarity.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our DL algorithm successfully differentiates NMR and MD structures which are indistinguishable on the free energy surface.
alignments.rationale: The publication emphasizes the DL algorithm's ability to differentiate structures but does not report the specific 'three clusters' result from the preprint, changing the claim from a specific clustering outcome to a more general discrimination capability.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our results demonstrate a new integrated approach for characterizing structural ensembles of intrinsically disordered proteins.
alignments.pub_claim_text: We demonstrate an integrated method to elucidate the structural ensemble of a complex formed by two IDRs.
alignments.rationale: Both describe demonstration of a new integrated approach for characterizing IDR-containing complexes; the publication restates this methodological conclusion.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Selective deuteration provides additional information that helps characterize structural ensembles.
alignments.rationale: The publication includes a specific claim about selective deuteration's benefit that was not explicitly present in the preprint.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Among the four force fields, a99SB-disp and CHARMM36m show the strongest agreement with SANS and NMR experiments.
alignments.rationale: The publication adds specific benchmarking results identifying particular force fields, which were not reported in the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: SAINT-Angle is a highly accurate method for predicting protein backbone torsion angles using a self-attention based deep learning network.
alignments.pub_claim_text: SAINT-Angle is a highly accurate method for predicting protein backbone torsion angles using a self-attention-based deep learning network.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same methodological accuracy claim with equivalent wording and scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed method achieved notable improvements over the best alternate methods in predicting backbone angles.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed self-attention-based network, together with transfer learning, has achieved notable improvements over the best alternate methods.
alignments.rationale: The publication ties the observed improvements explicitly to the combination of self-attention and transfer learning, clarifying the contributing factors.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: SAINT-Angle utilizes transfer learning to enhance the prediction of backbone angles.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed self-attention-based network, together with transfer learning, has achieved notable improvements over the best alternate methods.
alignments.rationale: The publication explicitly links transfer learning to performance gains, which directly aligns with the preprint's claim about using transfer learning.

alignments.pub_claim_text: SAINT-Angle was evaluated against state-of-the-art methods through an extensive study on benchmark datasets.
alignments.rationale: The publication adds an explicit statement about extensive benchmarking that was not separately claimed in the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: RISER reduced the read counts of dominant messenger and mitochondrial RNA classes by more than 85%.
alignments.pub_claim_text: RISER reduces the read counts of dominant messenger and mitochondrial RNA classes by more than 85%.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same quantitative reduction claim with equivalent wording and meaning.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: RISER increased sequencing depth of long non-coding RNAs by up to 93%.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The use of RISER results in an average increase in sequencing depth of 47% for long non-coding RNAs.
alignments.rationale: The preprint reported a maximum ('up to 93%') while the publication reports an average (47%), representing a tempered and revised quantitative claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: RISER achieved a decrease in globin reads by more than 90% in whole blood.
alignments.pub_claim_text: RISER achieves a decrease in globin reads by more than 90% and an increase in non-globin reads by 16% on average.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the >90% globin-decrease claim and adds additional detail about non-globin read increases, clarifying the overall effect.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: RISER is faster than GPU-accelerated basecalling and mapping.
alignments.pub_claim_text: RISER is faster than GPU-accelerated basecalling and mapping when using a GPU or a CPU.
alignments.rationale: The publication clarifies the comparison by specifying that RISER is faster both when using a GPU or a CPU, broadening the original, less-specified claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: RISER's software is modular and retrainable, allowing easy adaptation to other RNA classes.
alignments.rationale: The publication does not report the modular/retrainable software claim present in the preprint, so this claim appears to have been omitted.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We successfully predict consensus molecular subtypes in metastatic colorectal cancer patients using deep learning algorithms applied to the deconvolved transcriptomes of RNA within plasma extracellular vesicles.
alignments.pub_claim_text: We successfully predicted consensus molecular subtypes in patients with metastatic colorectal cancer using deep learning algorithms on plasma extracellular vesicle RNA.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the subtype-prediction result but omits the explicit 'deconvolved' phrasing; otherwise the claim is the same.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our approach demonstrates the ability to monitor changes in transcriptomic subtype under treatment selection pressure and identify molecular pathways in evRNA associated with recurrence.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Analysis of plasma evRNA enabled monitoring of changes in transcriptomic subtype under treatment selection pressure and identification of molecular pathways associated with recurrence.
alignments.rationale: The publication conveys the same monitoring and pathway-identification capabilities described in the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The results demonstrate the feasibility of transcriptomic-based liquid biopsy platforms for precision oncology approaches, enabling monitoring of tumor subtype changes and identification of expressed fusions and neoantigens.
alignments.pub_claim_text: This approach revealed expressed gene fusions and neoepitopes from evRNA.
alignments.rationale: The publication explicitly highlights the identification of expressed fusions and neoepitopes, which matches the preprint's claim about detecting fusions and neoantigens; wording is slightly different but meaning is preserved.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Serial analyses of tumor-encoded transcriptomes in liquid biopsies can enable facile cancer detection and monitor for recurrences and therapy-induced tumor evolution.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The development of an approach to interrogate molecular subtypes through RNA sequencing of plasma extracellular vesicles lays the foundation for liquid biopsy-based longitudinal monitoring of patient tumor transcriptomes.
alignments.rationale: The publication reframes the serial-analysis claim as laying a foundation for longitudinal monitoring, which clarifies and institutionalizes the preprint's proposed application.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Plasma RNA analysis can unleash tremendous opportunities for tumor state interrogation and molecular subtyping.
alignments.rationale: The publication includes a broad, high-level contribution statement about plasma RNA opportunities that is not explicitly present as a distinct claim in the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: AlphaFold2 predicted structures of fifteen plant proteomes provide a reliable resource for functional and evolutionary analysis of cysteine residues. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: AlphaFold2 predicted structures of fifteen plant proteomes provide a reliable basis for analyzing cysteine residues in the plant kingdom. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same contribution with near-identical meaning (reliable resource/basis for analyzing cysteines across the same proteomes).

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Most of the predicted disulfides from AlphaFold2 structures are trustworthy due to their high agreement (~96%) with those present in X-ray and NMR protein structures. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Most of the predicted disulfides from AlphaFold2 structures are trustworthy due to their high agreement (∼96%) with those present in X-ray and NMR protein structures. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The result is repeated verbatim in the publication with the same metric and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Zinc binding sites are increasingly present in plants at the expense of iron-sulfur clusters. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Zinc binding sites are increasingly present in plants at the expense of iron-sulfur clusters. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication contains the same conclusion with equivalent wording and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Disulfide formation is increased in secreted proteins of land plants, likely promoting sequence evolution to adapt to changing environments. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Disulfide formation is increased in secreted proteins of land plants, likely promoting sequence evolution to adapt to changing environments. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this conclusion with essentially identical wording and level of certainty.

alignments.pub_claim_text: AlphaFold2 predicted structural models are a rich source of information for studying the role of cysteine residues in proteins and for protein redox biology. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: This broader statement about AlphaFold2 models as a resource for redox biology does not appear in the preprint and represents an additional contribution in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed deep learning approach utilizes Relation BioBERT (R-BioBERT) to detect and classify drug-drug interactions (DDIs). (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed method not only determines whether two drugs interact but also identifies the specific types of interactions between them. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: Both claims state that the method detects and classifies (identifies types of) drug-drug interactions; the publication frames this as a contribution but keeps the same core claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Employing Bidirectional Long-Short Term Memory (BLSTM) significantly increases the prediction quality of DDI detection. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed method leveraging Relation BioBERT and Bidirectional Long Short-Term Memory improves the accuracy of predicting drug-drug interactions. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication attributes improved prediction accuracy to the combined method including BLSTM, matching the preprint's claim that BLSTM increases prediction quality.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Using BLSTM can significantly increase the F-scores compared to the baseline model on the SemEval 2013, TAC 2018, and TAC 2019 DDI Extraction datasets. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The use of BLSTM leads to significantly higher F-scores compared to the baseline model. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication keeps the same result about BLSTM producing higher F-scores but omits dataset specifics, effectively presenting a more general statement (broadened applicability).

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Detecting interactions between drugs is essential to prevent adverse effects and severe diseases in patients. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The general public-health motivation about preventing adverse effects is not represented among the publication claims, so this preprint claim appears omitted in the final paper.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: sc-CGconv works with substantially smaller sample sizes to identify homogeneous clusters. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: sc-CGconv works with substantially smaller sample sizes to identify homogeneous clusters. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this result verbatim with identical scope and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: sc-CGconv can model the expression co-variability of a large number of genes, thereby outperforming state-of-the-art gene selection/extraction methods for clustering. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: sc-CGconv can model the expression co-variability of a large number of genes, thereby outperforming state-of-the-art gene selection/extraction methods for clustering. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication contains the same performance claim with equivalent wording and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: sc-CGconv preserves the cell-to-cell variability within the selected gene set by constructing a cell-cell graph through copula correlation measure. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: sc-CGconv preserves the cell-to-cell variability within the selected gene set by constructing a cell-cell graph through copula correlation measure. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Identical methodological/result claim present in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: sc-CGconv provides a topology-preserving embedding of cells in low dimensional space. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: sc-CGconv provides a topology-preserving embedding of cells in low dimensional space. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates this result unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The Boolean network explorer (BoNE) enables prioritization of high-value targets in drug discovery for Inflammatory Bowel Disease (IBD). (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The Boolean network explorer (BoNE) enables the prioritization of high-value targets in drug discovery for Inflammatory Bowel Disease (IBD). (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same contribution claim with equivalent wording and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Balanced agonism of PPARα/γ was predicted to ameliorate colitis and reset the gene expression network from disease to health. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Balanced agonism of PPARα/γ was predicted to modulate macrophage processes and ameliorate colitis. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication shifts the mechanistic claim from a broad 'reset gene expression network' to a more specific modulation of macrophage processes and retains amelioration of colitis; this narrows and revises the original mechanistic claim and slightly tempers the broader systems-level wording.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Balanced dual agonism promotes bacterial clearance more efficiently than individual agonists in both in vivo and in vitro models. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Balanced dual agonism promotes bacterial clearance more efficiently than individual agonists, both in vivo and in vitro. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The experimental result is preserved verbatim in the publication with the same contexts and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Dual agonism reversed the defective bacterial clearance observed in PBMCs derived from IBD patients. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Dual agonism reversed the defective bacterial clearance observed in PBMCs derived from IBD patients. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The result is present in the publication with the same content but the stated certainty is reduced from high to medium, indicating a tempered confidence.

alignments.pub_claim_text: These findings highlight the potential of BoNE to rationalize combination therapy in IBD. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: This higher-level implication about rationalizing combination therapy does not appear in the preprint claims and is introduced as an additional conclusion in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: AI-based approaches can enhance the efficiency of molecular simulations by extracting relevant information from raw data. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: AI-based approaches can enhance the efficiency of molecular simulations by using AI derived slow modes to accelerate the simulation. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication makes the general preprint claim more specific by identifying 'AI-derived slow modes' as the mechanism of acceleration, narrowing the original, broader statement.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The use of AI in molecular simulations can lead to incorrect characterization of the reaction coordinate due to limited data and potential spurious solutions. (certainty=high, type=limitation)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The use of AI in molecular simulations can lead to incorrect characterization of the reaction coordinate due to limited data and potential spurious solutions. (certainty=high, type=limitation)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains this limitation identically with the same emphasis and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A novel and automated algorithm has been developed that maximizes the time-scale separation between slow and fast processes to improve AI solutions in molecular simulations. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A novel and automated algorithm has been developed that maximizes the timescale separation between slow and fast processes to provide more reliable AI-solutions. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication refines wording to emphasize reliability of AI solutions rather than the generic 'improve', but the underlying algorithmic claim remains the same.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The automatic protocol has been shown to be applicable to three classic benchmark problems in molecular simulations. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed automatic protocol has been shown to be applicable to three classic benchmark problems in molecular simulations. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates this demonstrative result with equivalent wording and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The work is expected to lead to increased and robust use of trustworthy AI in molecular simulations of complex systems. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The work aims to increase the robust use of trustworthy AI in molecular simulations of complex systems. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication slightly adjusts phrasing from 'is expected to lead to' to 'aims to', a modest clarification of intent/goals without changing the overall scope or certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study identifies both modality-specific and shared neural networks involved in Mental Imagery. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The study identifies both modality-specific and shared neural networks involved in Mental Imagery. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Text and meaning are effectively identical between preprint and publication; no change in scope or expressed confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Each form of Imagery preferentially recruited ‘higher level’ associative brain regions involved in the associated ‘real’ experience. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Each form of Imagery preferentially recruits 'higher level' associative brain regions involved in the associated 'real' experience. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Claim wording and meaning are the same; tense change does not alter content or certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: There is significant overlap in a left-lateralized network including the pre-supplementary motor area, ventral premotor cortex, and inferior parietal lobule. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: There is significant overlap in a left-lateralized network including the pre-supplementary motor area, ventral premotor cortex, and inferior parietal lobule. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Identical claim in content and certainty between versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The results support the existence of a ‘core’ network that supports the attentional, spatial, and decision-making demands of Mental Imagery. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The results support the existence of a 'core' network that supports the attentional, spatial, and decision-making demands of Mental Imagery. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Conclusion and level of certainty are preserved exactly in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The findings offer new insights into the brain networks underlying human imagination. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The findings offer new insights into the brain networks underlying human imagination. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: High overlap in content and same expressed certainty; no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Maladaptive behaviors that characterize neuropsychiatric disorders are most proximally determined by networks of neurons. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Maladaptive behaviors that characterize neuropsychiatric disorders are most proximally determined by networks of neurons. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Claim wording and certainty are the same in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: It was possible to predict personalized network modifications that substantially reduced maladaptive behavior without inducing side effects. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: It was possible to predict personalized network modifications that substantially reduced maladaptive behavior without inducing side effects. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Substantively identical result claim and unchanged certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Information about neural activity during certain types of trials was more helpful in predicting treatment than information about model parameters. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Information about neural activity during certain types of trials was more helpful in predicting treatment than information about model parameters. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Claim content and expressed certainty remain the same across versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This computational study lays the groundwork for future studies utilizing more biologically realistic network models in conjunction with in vivo data. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: This computational study lays the groundwork for future studies utilizing more biologically realistic network models in conjunction with in vivo data. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: Content of the contribution is unchanged but the publication reports a lower certainty level (high -> medium), indicating tempered confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: At least half of the CNN depth was required to match the modulation of rat classification accuracy in tasks where objects underwent variations of size, position and orientation. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Rat ability to discriminate objects despite scaling, translation, and rotation was well accounted for by the CNN mid-level layers. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Publication reframes the same result by emphasizing mid-level layers (clarifying which portion of depth was responsible) while preserving the overall claim and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The full network was needed to equal the tolerance of rat perception to more severe image manipulations, such as partial occlusion and reduction of objects to their outlines. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The tolerance displayed by rats to more severe image manipulations was achieved by the network only in the final layers. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication changes the locus of the effect from the 'full network' to 'final layers' (a substantive revision that narrows the claimed network components responsible).

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Rats displayed a perceptual strategy that was way more invariant than that of the CNN, as they more consistently relied on the same set of diagnostic features across object transformations. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Rats deployed perceptual strategies that were more invariant than those of the CNN. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Publication retains the core claim about greater invariance but omits the detailed mechanistic phrasing about diagnostic features and reports a lower certainty, so the claim is clarified and confidence tempered.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: These results reveal an unexpected level of sophistication of rat object vision. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: These results reveal an unexpected level of sophistication of rat object vision. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The conclusion's wording is identical but the publication expresses higher certainty (medium -> high), so confidence is amplified.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Despite their proficiency in solving challenging image classification tasks, CNNs learn solutions that only marginally match those of biological visual systems. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: CNNs learn solutions that only marginally match those of biological visual systems. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Core conclusion is preserved with only stylistic trimming; content and certainty unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The lead Nanoligomer™ identified in a screen of human donor derived peripheral blood mononuclear cells (PBMCs) upregulated Erythropoietin (EPO) and showed the greatest reversal of radiation induced cytokine changes. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The lead Nanoligomer™ identified in a screen of human donor derived peripheral blood mononuclear cells (PBMCs) upregulated Erythropoietin (EPO) and showed the greatest reversal of radiation induced cytokine changes. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Identical result claim and certainty between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The method used in this study alters related gene network targets ultimately resulting in pathway modulation. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The method used in this study alters related gene network targets ultimately resulting in pathway modulation. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Claim content and expressed certainty are maintained.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The in vivo testing in a mouse radiation-model demonstrated that the lead Nanoligomer™ regulated gene expression of epo in lung tissue and countered immune suppression. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The lead molecule was shown to regulate gene expression of epo in lung tissue as well as counter immune suppression in a mouse radiation-model. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Substantively the same in vivo result reported; slight wording differences do not change meaning or certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This approach to high-throughput drug-discovery has broader applicability towards drug-discovery and potential for further investigation as reversible gene therapy to treat adverse health outcomes induced by radiation exposure. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The approach to high-throughput drug-discovery and target validation has broader applicability towards drug-discovery. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Publication preserves the claim of broader applicability for drug-discovery but omits the specific suggestion about reversible gene therapy and treatment of radiation-induced outcomes, narrowing the contribution.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: CellDissect optimizes specimen preparation and data acquisition to generate high quality images that are easier to analyze computationally. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: CellDissect optimizes specimen preparation and data acquisition to generate high quality images that are easier to analyze computationally. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: Contribution statement is identical in wording and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: CellDissect relies only on widefield images and nuclear staining to automatically segment cells in two dimensions and nuclei in three dimensions. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: CellDissect relies only on widefield images and nuclear staining to automatically segment cells in two dimensions and nuclei in three dimensions. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Result claim unchanged between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The accuracy of different nuclear segmentation approaches is compared and evaluated against manual expert cell segmentation for various cell lines and imaging modalities. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The accuracy of different nuclear segmentation approaches was compared and evaluated against manual expert cell segmentation for various cell lines and imaging modalities. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Only tense changed from present to past; substantive content and certainty are the same.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The lack of standardisation among reference image catalogues has led to problems with observer bias and the inability to combine datasets across studies. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The lack of standardisation among reference image catalogues has led to problems with observer bias and the inability to combine datasets across studies. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication text is essentially identical to the preprint claim in content and strength, so no substantive change is detected.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We propose a database structure to facilitate standardisation of morphospecies image catalogues between research groups. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: We propose a database structure to facilitate standardisation of morphospecies image catalogues between research groups. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same proposal without substantive modification.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We propose a framework for coordination of international efforts to develop reference guides for the identification of marine species from images. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: We propose a framework for coordination of international efforts to develop reference guides for the identification of marine species from images. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication claim matches the preprint claim verbatim in purpose and strength.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We identify a mechanism by which overall quality of data within a common reference guide could be raised over the next decade. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: We identify a mechanism by which overall quality of data within a common reference guide could be raised over the next decade. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the mechanism-identification claim with equivalent scope and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A common reference standard is essential for advancing marine ecology and supporting sustainable use of this ecosystem. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A common reference standard is essential for advancing marine ecology and supporting sustainable use of this ecosystem. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The conclusion about the importance of a common reference standard is unchanged in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The 24H-NBOMe(F) and 34H-NBOMe(F) compounds reduced despair-like behavior in zebrafish. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The 24H-NBOMe(F) and 34H-NBOMe(F) treatments reduced zebrafish despair-like behavior. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Functional analysis implicates potential involvement of serotonin release stimulating activity and various psychiatric and neurologic disorder treatment activities. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Two main axes where encoding scores are significant emerged in bilateral temporo-parietal electrodes first (200-500ms) and frontal and posterior central electrodes later (500-700ms). (certainty=high, type=result)

(certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.rationale: The publication retains this concluding suggestion about candidate lncRNAs with similar certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Embarcadero is an R package designed for species distribution modelling using Bayesian additive regression trees (BART). (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Embarcadero is an R package designed for species distribution modeling using Bayesian additive regression trees (BART). (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The core contribution claim about the package and BART usage is unchanged between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: BART has not been previously applied to species distribution modelling. (certainty=high, type=limitation)
alignments.pub_claim_text: BART has not been previously applied to species distribution modeling. (certainty=high, type=limitation)
alignments.rationale: The limitation/novelty statement is carried forward without substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Embarcadero includes functionality for easy spatial prediction and automated variable selection. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Embarcadero includes tools for spatial prediction, automated variable selection, and partial dependence visualization. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication expands the described functionality by adding partial dependence visualization, clarifying and broadening the original feature list.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The package is intended for eventual release on CRAN and is available open source on Github. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The package is open source and available on GitHub. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication omits the stated intention to release on CRAN and only affirms GitHub availability, narrowing the distribution-related claim and tempering the planned-release aspect.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A BART workflow for a virtual species distribution model is illustrated to demonstrate the use of embarcadero. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A BART workflow for a virtual species distribution model is illustrated to demonstrate the use of embarcadero. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The illustrative workflow example is presented in both versions with equivalent scope and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Half the Ot genome is composed of repetitive DNA, and there is minimal collinearity in gene order between strains. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Half the Orientia tsutsugamushi genome is composed of repetitive DNA, and there is minimal collinearity in gene order between strains. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The content is the same in both versions; the publication reduces stated certainty (high→medium), so semantic meaning is unchanged but confidence is tempered.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We found evidence for wide-spread post-transcriptional antisense regulation in Ot. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The study finds evidence for wide-spread post-transcriptional antisense regulation in Orientia tsutsugamushi. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The claim is carried forward with equivalent wording and certainty; no substantive change in scope or confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Distinct immune response networks are up-regulated in response to each clinical isolate of Ot, leading to predictions of relative virulence which were confirmed in a mouse infection model. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Distinct immune response networks are identified for two clinical isolates of Orientia tsutsugamushi, leading to predictions of relative virulence validated in a mouse infection model. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication specifies two isolates whereas the preprint phrased responses to each isolate more generally; the main result is preserved but narrowed to two isolates (clarification of scope).

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Dual RNA-seq can provide insight into the biology and host-pathogen interactions of a poorly characterized and genetically intractable organism such as Ot. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Dual RNA-seq provides insights into host-pathogen interactions, particularly for intracellular organisms. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication generalizes the conclusion to intracellular organisms (broader scope) and states it with higher certainty; the core claim about dual RNA-seq providing insights is retained but broadened and amplified.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Longer CTG expansions in DM1 are associated with greater symptom severity and earlier age at onset. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Longer CTG expansions in DM1 are associated with greater symptom severity and earlier age at onset. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Direct one-to-one carryover with identical wording and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The primary mechanism of pathogenesis in DM1 is mediated by a gain of function of the CUG-containing RNA. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The primary mechanism of pathogenesis in DM1 is mediated by a gain of function of the CUG-containing RNA. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Claim is unchanged between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Alternative splicing and alternative polyadenylation of many genes are disrupted in DM1. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Alternative splicing and alternative polyadenylation of many genes are disrupted in DM1. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Direct match with no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Candidate mRNA biomarkers can be used to model an effective reduction in CTG length in DM1. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Candidate mRNA biomarkers can be used to model an effective reduction in CTG length in DM1. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: Same claim and framing in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model developed can be used to detect treatment effects in the context of a clinical trial for DM1. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The model developed can be used to detect treatment effects in the context of a clinical trial for DM1. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: Claim content is preserved; publication labels it as a contribution but the substantive claim is the same.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Many commonly used phylogenetic models can be implemented using a probabilistic programming language. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Many commonly used phylogenetic models can be implemented using a probabilistic programming language. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: Direct match in content and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Black box variational inference in Stan is less accurate than MCMC methods for phylogenetic models. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Black box variational inference in Stan is less accurate than MCMC methods for phylogenetic models. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Exact replication of the result claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Black box variational inference in Stan requires far less compute time than MCMC methods. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Black box variational inference in Stan requires far less compute time than MCMC methods. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: No substantive change between versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A specialized implementation of variational inference can be two orders of magnitude faster and more accurate than a general purpose probabilistic implementation. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A specialized implementation of variational inference can be two orders of magnitude faster and more accurate than a general purpose probabilistic implementation. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Claim retained verbatim with same emphasis.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: HiPR-FISH can distinguish 1023 E. coli strains, each fluorescently labeled with a unique binary barcode. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: HiPR-FISH can distinguish between 1,023 isolates of Escherichia coli, each fluorescently labelled with a unique binary barcode. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Same technical result with equivalent numeric detail and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: HiPR-FISH reveals the intricate spatial architectures formed by bacteria in the human oral plaque microbiome. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: HiPR-FISH demonstrates the longitudinal stability of spatial architectures in the human oral plaque microbiome. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication emphasizes longitudinal stability rather than a general characterization of 'intricate architectures', indicating a shift in the specific result focus (revised/narrowed).

alignments.preprint_claim_text: HiPR-FISH shows disruption of spatial networks in the mouse gut microbiome in response to antibiotic treatment. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: HiPR-FISH reveals the disruption of spatial networks in the mouse gut microbiome in response to treatment with antibiotics. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Direct match in content and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: HiPR-FISH provides a framework for analyzing the spatial organization of microbial communities at single cell resolution. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: HiPR-FISH provides a framework for analysing the spatial ecology of environmental microbial communities at single-cell resolution. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication narrows the framing to environmental microbial communities and strengthens certainty (medium→high), clarifying and narrowing the original broader claim.

alignments.pub_claim_text: HiPR-FISH shows the diverse strategies of ribosome organization exhibited by taxa in the human oral microbiome. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: This specific result about ribosome organization in the oral microbiome appears in the publication but was not present in the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The machine learning classifier was able to reliably classify sex based on spatially specific resting state brain-connectivity across independent samples. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The classifier was able to reliably classify sex based on spatially specific resting state brain connectivity. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Same result and certainty; wording streamlined but meaning unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Brain regions with the highest sex classification accuracies were primarily located along the cingulate cortex, medial and lateral frontal cortex, temporo-parietal regions, insula, and precuneus. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Brain regions displaying the highest sex classification accuracies were mainly located along the cingulate cortex, medial and lateral frontal cortex, temporoparietal regions, insula, and precuneus. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Content and certainty preserved; minor wording changes do not alter meaning.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The identified brain regions displaying sex differences in connectivity are stable across samples and align with previously described sex differences in functional brain organization. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The areas of high classification accuracy were stable across samples and match well with previously described sex differences in functional brain organization. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Same conclusion with equivalent certainty and scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The data do not support a clear-cut dimorphism in functional brain organization that is driven by sex alone. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The data do not support a clear-cut dimorphism in functional brain organization that is driven by sex alone. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Identical conclusion carried through to the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: nano-ID is a method that detects newly synthesized RNA isoforms and monitors isoform metabolism.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Nano-ID is a method that detects newly synthesized RNA isoforms and monitors isoform metabolism.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same methodological contribution with equivalent scope and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Application of nano-ID to the heat shock response in human cells reveals that many RNA isoforms change their synthesis rate, stability, and splicing pattern.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Application of nano-ID to the heat shock response in human cells reveals that many RNA isoforms change their stability.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the heat-shock application but reports changes in stability only, omitting synthesis rate and splicing, thus narrowing and weakening the original multicomponent claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The metabolism of individual RNA isoforms differs strongly from that estimated for the combined RNA signal at a specific gene locus.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Nano-ID shows that the metabolism of individual RNA isoforms differs strongly from that estimated for the combined RNA signal at a specific gene locus.
alignments.rationale: The publication conveys the same result about isoform-specific metabolism with equivalent wording and emphasis.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Combined RNA stability correlates with poly(A)-tail length, but individual RNA isoforms can deviate significantly.
alignments.rationale: No corresponding statement about poly(A)-tail length and isoform deviations appears in the publication; the claim was omitted.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Machine learning techniques can help understand the latent biological mechanism of action from biomedical data.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Machine learning techniques can help understand the latent biological mechanism of action in biomedical data.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same high-level conclusion with equivalent meaning and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Unsupervised generative neural methods based on the variational autoencoder can model cell differentiation by building meaningful representations from gene expression data.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Unsupervised generative neural methods based on the variational autoencoder can model cell differentiation by building meaningful representations from complex gene expression data.
alignments.rationale: The publication adds the adjective 'complex' to describe the data, a minor clarification without changing scope or confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Disentanglement methods based on information theory improve data representation and achieve better separation of biological factors of variation in gene expression data.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Disentanglement methods based on information theory improve data representation and achieve better separation of biological factors of variation in gene expression data.
alignments.rationale: The result is carried forward verbatim with equivalent framing and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A computational framework was developed to identify cell types, find driver genes for differentiation, and understand relationships between cells.
alignments.pub_claim_text: A computational framework was developed for identifying cell types, finding driver genes for differentiation, and understanding relationships between cells.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the contribution with equivalent content and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Humans can implicitly learn complex perceptuo-motor skills over the course of large numbers of trials.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Humans can implicitly learn complex perceptuo-motor skills over the course of large numbers of trials.
alignments.rationale: The main conclusion is preserved identically in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Participants began to rely on two previous elements (i.e., trigrams) in the first session, successfully adapting to the higher-order structure in the task.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Participants began to rely on two previous elements (i.e., trigrams) in the first session, adapting to the most prominent higher-order structure in the task.
alignments.rationale: The publication refines wording to emphasize the most prominent structure, a small clarification without changing the substantive result.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The influence of local statistical fluctuations on participants’ responses waned over subsequent sessions as subjects evidenced skilled performance.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The extent to which local statistical fluctuations in trigram frequency influenced participants' responses waned over subsequent sessions.
alignments.rationale: The publication specifies 'trigram frequency' and frames the effect as an extent that waned, a clarifying rephrasing of the original result.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: By the eighth session, a subset of participants shifted their prior to consider a context deeper than two previous elements.
alignments.pub_claim_text: By the eighth session, a subset of participants shifted their prior to consider a context deeper than two previous elements.
alignments.rationale: This specific result is preserved verbatim in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model offers the first principled account of the adaptive complexity and nuanced dynamics of humans’ internal sequence representations during long-term implicit skill learning.
alignments.rationale: The publication does not include the explicit claim that the model is the 'first principled account'; instead it adds an empirical result absent from the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: RegVelo demonstrates superior predictive power for interactions and perturbation simulations compared to methods that focus solely on dynamics or GRN inference.
alignments.pub_claim_text: RegVelo provides reliable predictive power for terminal states, gene interactions, and perturbation simulations across diverse biological systems.
alignments.rationale: The publication tones down the comparative 'superior' claim to a statement of reliable predictive power and broadens the claimed applicability across terminal states and systems, reducing the comparative emphasis.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Using RegVelo’s in silico perturbation predictions, we establish transcription factor tfec as an early driver and elf1 as a novel regulator of pigment cell fate.
alignments.pub_claim_text: tfec is established as an early driver and elf1 as a regulator of pigment cell fate through in silico perturbations and validation by CRISPR-Cas9 knockout and single-cell Perturb-seq.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the finding but adds experimental validation (CRISPR and Perturb-seq), which clarifies and strengthens confidence in the regulatory roles.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We propose a gene-regulatory circuit involving tfec and elf1 interactions via the toggle-switch model.
alignments.rationale: No publication claim explicitly presents the specific toggle-switch circuit proposal; the paper emphasizes broader frameworks and validated regulators instead.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This study introduces a novel discovery pipeline that combines protein language models with a structural representation tree to identify enzymes based on structural similarity.
alignments.pub_claim_text: VenusMine identifies and clusters target proteins based on structural similarity using sequence information.
alignments.rationale: The publication presents the same discovery capability under the name VenusMine and slightly different emphasis (sequence information vs explicit model+tree), a clarified framing of the same contribution.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Biochemical experiments showed that 14 of 34 candidates exhibited PET degradation activity across a temperature range of 30–60 °C.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Fourteen candidates exhibit PET degradation activity across 30–60 °C.
alignments.rationale: The reported experimental result is carried forward identically.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Kb PETase possesses a melting temperature 32 °C higher than that of Is PETase and exhibits the highest PET degradation activity within 30–50 °C compared to other wild-type PETases.
alignments.pub_claim_text: KbPETase demonstrates a melting temperature (Tm) 32 °C higher than IsPETase and exhibits the highest PET degradation activity within 30 – 65 °C among wild-type PETases.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the Tm difference but expands the reported activity temperature range (30–65 °C) and frames the comparison across wild-type PETases, broadening the original claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Kb PETase shows higher catalytic efficiency than FastPETase.
alignments.pub_claim_text: KbPETase surpasses FastPETase and LCC in catalytic efficiency.
alignments.rationale: The publication expands the comparison to include LCC and thus clarifies and strengthens the claim about KbPETase's catalytic efficiency.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This work develops a novel deep learning approach to discover natural PETases with enhanced functions.
alignments.pub_claim_text: This work demonstrates a novel deep learning approach for discovering natural PETases with enhanced properties.
alignments.rationale: The publication reframes the contribution as a demonstrated approach and increases certainty, while preserving the core claim about a deep learning discovery method.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Enhancer turnover occurs in approximately 6% of the whole genome, with more than 80% of the novel activity arising from repurposing of enhancers between cell-types. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Enhancer turnover occurs in approximately 6% of the whole genome, with more than 80% of the novel activity arising from repurposing of enhancers between cell types. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same quantitative result and interpretation with no substantive change in scope or confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The frequency of enhancer turnover in a cell-type is remarkably similar across three evolutionary pathways, despite only ∼19% overlap in the source regions. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Flanking enhancers of neurodevelopmental genes undergo high turnover irrespective of the mutational model pathway, suggesting a high plasticity in neurocognitive evolution. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication reframes the observation specifically for flanking enhancers of neurodevelopmental genes and interprets it as plasticity in neurocognitive evolution, removing the broader three-pathway comparison and the explicit 19% overlap statement.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Enhancers predisposed to turnover display reduced evolutionary constraints and are depleted in GWAS variants. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: No corresponding statement about reduced evolutionary constraint or GWAS variant depletion appears in the publication claims.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Most enhancers undergoing turnover occur within 100kb of a gene, with the highest turnover near neurodevelopmental genes including CNTNAP2, NPAS3, and AUTS2. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The majority of turnover loci are localized within 100 kb of a gene, with the highest turnover occurring near neurodevelopmental genes including CNTNAP2, NPAS3, and AUTS2. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same genomic proximity and specific gene examples with equivalent certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Enhancer repurposing within vulnerable loci drives regulatory innovation while keeping the core regulatory networks intact. (certainty=low, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Enhancer repurposing within vulnerable loci drives regulatory innovation while keeping the core regulatory networks intact. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the same conclusion but reports it with higher stated certainty, effectively amplifying confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: PIPENN-EMB demonstrates substantial improvement over the previously published PIPENN model for protein interaction interface prediction, reaching an MCC of 0.313 vs. 0.249, and AUC-ROC 0.800 vs. 0.755 on the BIO_DL_TE test set. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: PIPENN-EMB demonstrates substantial improvement over the previously published PIPENN model for protein interaction interface prediction, reaching an MCC of 0.313 vs. 0.249. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the MCC improvement but omits the AUC-ROC comparison, altering the numeric claims while preserving the main improvement assertion.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: PIPENN-EMB reaches state-of-the-art performance on the ZK448 dataset for protein-protein interface prediction. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: PIPENN-EMB achieves state-of-the-art performance on the ZK448 dataset for protein-protein interface prediction. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same performance claim with equivalent certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: PIPENN-EMB generalises to remote homologs, yielding stable AUC-ROC across all three test sets with less than 30% sequence identity to the training dataset, and even to proteins with less than 15% sequence identity. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: PIPENN-EMB generalises to remote homologs, yielding stable AUROC across all three test sets with less than 30% sequence identity to the training dataset. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication keeps the <30% identity generalization claim but drops the more extreme assertion about performance at <15% identity, narrowing and tempering the original claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The embeddings used in PIPENN-EMB cover a broad range of ‘hand-crafted’ protein features in ablation studies. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The embeddings used in PIPENN-EMB cover a broad range of ‘hand-crafted’ protein features in ablation studies. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the ablation-study conclusion with no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: RiboDecode demonstrates robust predictive accuracy for unseen genes and cellular environments. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: RiboDecode demonstrates robust predictive accuracy for unseen genes and cellular environments. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same performance claim with equivalent wording and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: In vitro experiments showed substantial improvements in protein expression, significantly outperforming past methods. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: In vitro experiments showed substantial improvements in protein expression, significantly outperforming past methods. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The in vitro result is presented identically in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: In vivo mouse studies showed that optimized influenza hemagglutinin mRNAs induce ten times stronger neutralizing antibody responses against influenza virus compared to the unoptimized sequence. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: In vivo mouse studies showed that optimized influenza hemagglutinin mRNAs induce ten times stronger neutralizing antibody responses against influenza virus compared to the unoptimized sequence. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication reproduces the quantitative in vivo finding verbatim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Optimized nerve growth factor mRNAs achieve equivalent neuroprotection of retinal ganglion cells at one-fifth the dose of the unoptimized sequence. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Optimized nerve growth factor mRNAs achieve equivalent neuroprotection of retinal ganglion cells at one-fifth the dose of the unoptimized sequence. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the dose-sparing neuroprotection result with the same quantitative claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: RiboDecode represents a paradigm shift from rule-based to a data-driven, context-aware approach for mRNA therapeutic applications. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: RiboDecode represents a paradigm shift from rule-based to a data-driven, context-aware approach for mRNA therapeutic applications. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the conceptual claim about methodological paradigm without substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: It is possible to sequence antibody variable domains from cryoEM reconstructions with accuracies of up to 80-90%. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: It is possible to sequence antibody variable domains from cryoEM reconstructions with accuracies of up to 80–90%. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the high-accuracy sequencing claim verbatim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The ModelAngelo-derived sequences can be used to assign the used V-genes. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The ModelAngelo-derived sequences can be used to assign the used V-genes. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the same practical application claim for V-gene assignment.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This approach improves the tolerance to a background of polyclonal antibody sequences. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The approach improves the tolerance to a background of polyclonal antibody sequences. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same conclusion regarding tolerance to polyclonal backgrounds.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Map quality is a critical bottleneck in the sequencing of antibody variable domains. (certainty=medium, type=limitation)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Map quality is a critical bottleneck in sequencing antibody variable domains from cryoEM reconstructions. (certainty=medium, type=limitation)
alignments.rationale: The publication expresses the same limitation with equivalent emphasis.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The new mRNA design algorithm overcomes limitations of existing computational approaches for optimizing mRNA sequences. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The software supports complex RNA design procedures, including mRNA design with flexible objective functions. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication characterizes the contribution as software support for complex mRNA design workflows rather than the broader claim of outright overcoming all prior limitations, softening the original statement.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Major improvements to the original differentiable folding algorithm drastically reduce the memory overhead of the gradient calculation. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: JAX-RNAfold is an open-source software package that implements a differentiable folding algorithm capable of scaling to 1,250 nucleotides on a single GPU. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication provides a concrete demonstration (scaling to 1,250 nt) that clarifies and amplifies the preprint's claim about reduced memory overhead and algorithmic improvement.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The mRNA design problem is formulated in the context of continuous sequences, requiring the generalization of existing metrics. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication's listed claims do not mention formulation in continuous sequence space or metric generalization, so this conceptual claim does not appear.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A generative deep learning approach was developed that treats the differentiable folding algorithm as a module in a larger optimization pipeline. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: JAX-RNAfold allows for the integration of differentiable folding as a module in larger deep learning pipelines. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication emphasizes the software integration capability but does not assert the specific development of a generative model, thus clarifying and slightly reframing the preprint contribution.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The method optimizes mRNA sequences via complex, therapeutically relevant objective functions. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The software supports complex RNA design procedures, including mRNA design with flexible objective functions. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the core claim about support for complex objective functions in mRNA design but presents it as a software capability with slightly lower stated certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The RNA structural space is not complete even at the di-nucleotide level.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The structural space currently observed in RNA is far from complete.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the core claim that RNA structural space is incomplete but omits the specific 'even at di-nucleotide level' emphasis and reports lower certainty, so the claim is presented more generally and with tempered confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: An exponential increase of new structural fragments is observed at tetrameric and pentameric levels in RNA.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The number of non-redundant structural fragments at the tetra- and penta-nucleotide levels is in the midst of exponential increase.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same observation about exponential increase at tetra- and penta-nucleotide levels with similar certainty and wording.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The number of backbone fragments found in RNA (2510) is significantly smaller than that found in proteins (6652).
alignments.rationale: No corresponding count or explicit protein-vs-RNA fragment comparison appears in the publication claims, indicating this specific quantitative comparison was dropped.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The structural space currently observed in RNA is far from complete due to its greater degrees of freedom compared to proteins.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The structural space currently observed in RNA is far from complete.
alignments.rationale: The publication keeps the conclusion that RNA structural space is incomplete but removes the causal explanation (greater degrees of freedom compared to proteins), so the claim is presented more generally.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The three-atom representation has the lowest number of structural fragments, suggesting it as the most stable structural frame for RNA.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The reference frame composed of three sugar-ring atoms near the base side exhibits the least structural diversity among existing RNA structures.
alignments.rationale: The publication separates the empirical observation (least structural diversity for the three-atom frame) from the interpretive suggestion; the reported result aligns closely with the preprint's empirical component.

alignments.pub_claim_text: More concerted efforts are needed to improve the speed and methods of experimental determination of RNA structures.
alignments.rationale: This is a new contribution/recommendation in the publication not present among the preprint claims.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our method outperforms state-of-the-art techniques on diverse ARG datasets, including those with low homology to the training data.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The novel approach using ProteinBERT outperforms state-of-the-art techniques on diverse ARG datasets.
alignments.rationale: The publication asserts the same performance improvement on diverse ARG datasets with matching certainty and framing.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Attention analysis of the model reveals that it considers biologically relevant features, such as conserved amino acid residues and antibiotic target binding sites, when making predictions.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Attention analysis of the model reveals that it considers biologically relevant features when making predictions.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the attention-analysis claim but reports it with higher certainty; the core content (consideration of biologically relevant features) is the same, though the publication drops the specific examples in the concise claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: These findings provide valuable insights into the molecular basis of antibiotic resistance and demonstrate the interpretability of protein language models.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The findings provide valuable insights into the molecular basis of antibiotic resistance.
alignments.rationale: The publication keeps the insight-into-resistance claim but omits the explicit statement about demonstrating interpretability of protein language models and expresses slightly lower certainty, so the claim is presented more narrowly/tempered.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The method highlights its potential for predicting the resistance mechanisms of unknown ARGs.
alignments.rationale: This forward-looking application (predicting mechanisms of unknown ARGs) is introduced in the publication and was not stated among the preprint claims.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We present a VAE-based approach for accurately calculating conditional distributions in neural encoding and decoding.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The VAE-based approach presented effectively calculates conditional distributions in neural encoding and decoding.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the core methodological contribution with equivalent framing and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our approach demonstrates applicability to high-dimensional behavioral time series by retrieving conditional distributions over masked body parts of walking flies.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The method retrieves conditional distributions over masked body parts of walking flies.
alignments.rationale: The publication affirms the same experimental validation on walking flies, matching the preprint claim closely.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We probabilistically decode motor trajectories from neural population activity in a monkey reach task using our VAE-based approach.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The approach successfully decodes motor trajectories from neural activity in a monkey-reach task.
alignments.rationale: The publication directly affirms the same decoding result in the monkey reach task with comparable certainty.
