alignments.rationale: The same evaluation claim is reported but with substantially different (higher) accuracy and relative improvement in the publication, representing a revision and stronger reported performance.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The average per-target F1 measure score of the domain boundary prediction by DistDom is 0.263, which is 29.56% higher than the state-of-the-art method. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The average per-target F1 measure score of domain boundary prediction by DistDom is 0.263, which is 29.56% higher than the state-of-the-art method when tested on CASP14 multi-domain protein targets. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The numerical F1 and relative improvement are unchanged, but the publication clarifies that this result pertains specifically to CASP14 multi-domain targets, narrowing the stated scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: WzaX, WzaS, and WzaB are truncated OPX homologues of Escherichia coli Wza that lack OM-spanning α-helices. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The exopolysaccharide pathway WzaX, major spore coat pathway WzaS, and biosurfactant polysaccharide pathway WzaB in Myxococcus xanthus are truncated OPX homologues of Escherichia coli Wza. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Both assert these proteins are truncated OPX homologues; the publication omits explicit mention of lacking OM-spanning α-helices but does not contradict the structural identification, so the core claim stands.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: WzaX/S/B architecture is the most common among three defined OPX-protein structural classes, independent of periplasmic thickness. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Comparative genomics revealed that the truncated WzaX/S/B architecture is the most common among three defined OPX-protein structural classes across all bacteria. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the prevalence claim but frames it across all bacteria (broader taxonomic scope) and omits the explicit periplasmic-thickness independence, clarifying and broadening the finding.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: MXAN_3226 is essential for MASC export, and the absence of MXAN_7418 compromises EPS translocation. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: MXAN_3226 is essential for MASC export, and MXAN_7418 mediates EPS translocation, leading to the designation of a new secretion machinery component 'Wzp'. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication affirms MXAN_3226 function and more explicitly names MXAN_7418 as mediating EPS translocation and defines a new component name, clarifying and formalizing the original result.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The data support a novel secretion paradigm for Wzx/Wzy-dependent pathways that utilize a β-barrel porin to mediate polysaccharide transport across the OM. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The final step of Myxococcus xanthus EPS/MASC/BPS secretion across the outer membrane is proposed to be mediated by WzpX/S/B. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Both propose that a β-barrel porin mediates outer-membrane polysaccharide secretion; the publication names the mediators as WzpX/S/B, providing a clarified and named version of the proposed paradigm.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We predicted 1,798 protein-protein interactions for cancer driver proteins involved in diverse cellular processes. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: We predicted 1,798 protein-protein interactions for cancer driver proteins involved in diverse cellular processes. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The prediction count and scope are reproduced identically in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our predictions offer novel structural insight into many cancer-related processes such as the MAP kinase cascade and Fanconi anemia pathway. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our predictions offer novel structural insight into many cancer-related processes such as the MAP kinase cascade and Fanconi anemia pathway. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the same conclusion about providing novel structural insight into these pathways.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Interfaces enriched in mutations tend to function in pathways that are deregulated in cancers. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Interfaces enriched in mutations tend to function in pathways that are deregulated in cancers. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The claim about mutation-enriched interfaces and their pathway associations is unchanged in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Interfaces lacking mutations appear to be essential for the survival of cancer cells and thus may be future targets for PPI modulating drugs. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Interfaces lacking mutations appear to be essential for the survival of cancer cells and thus may be future targets for PPI modulating drugs. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same conclusion about mutation-lacking interfaces as potential therapeutic targets without substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The MEBRAINS template overcomes limitations of existing templates by using a combination of T1 and T2 images from the same 10 animals. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The MEBRAINS template overcomes limitations of existing macaque brain templates by using a combination of T1 and T2 images from the same 10 animals. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication claim is essentially identical in content and certainty to the preprint claim, describing the same methodological advantage and dataset.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: MEBRAINS aims to improve integration of multi-modal multi-scale macaque data. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: MEBRAINS aims to improve integration of multimodal multi-scale macaque data and is quantitatively equal to, or better than, currently widely used macaque templates. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the aim but adds a quantitative performance statement, making the original aim more specific and stated with higher certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The resulting volumetric T1 and T2 templates are supplemented with high quality white and gray matter surfaces built with FreeSurfer. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The published claim set does not mention FreeSurfer-generated surfaces or the specific surface resources present in the preprint; this specific claim is absent from the publication claims.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: MEBRAINS is quantitatively equal or better compared to currently widely used macaque templates. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: MEBRAINS aims to improve integration of multimodal multi-scale macaque data and is quantitatively equal to, or better than, currently widely used macaque templates. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the quantitative comparison claim verbatim (merged with the aims statement), keeping meaning and confidence intact.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The template is integrated in the EBRAINS and Scalable Brain Atlas web-based infrastructures. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication claims list provided does not mention integration into EBRAINS or Scalable Brain Atlas, so this infrastructure integration claim appears omitted.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The template is populated with a parcellation scheme based on cyto- and receptor architectonic analyses. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: This parcellation-detail claim does not appear among the preprint claims and thus is new in the publication.

alignments.pub_claim_text: MEBRAINS provides a detailed description of the algorithms/methods used to create the template to assist future researchers. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The methods-documentation emphasis is not present in the preprint claim list and appears as an additional publication claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Deep Local Analysis (DLA) accurately estimates the binding affinity change for associated complexes based on the two cubes associated with the wild-type and the mutant residues. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Deep Local Analysis (DLA) accurately estimates the binding affinity change for associated complexes based on the two cubes associated with the wild-type and the mutant residues. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the methodological accuracy claim verbatim with comparable certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: DLA achieves a Pearson correlation coefficient of 0.81 on more than 2,000 mutations, demonstrating its effectiveness. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: DLA achieves a Pearson correlation coefficient of 0.735 on about 400 mutations on unseen complexes. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The published result reports a lower correlation on a specific (unseen complexes) subset with fewer mutations, so the quantitative performance claim has been revised and narrowed in scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The generalization capability of DLA to unseen complexes is higher than that of state-of-the-art methods. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The generalization capability of DLA on blind datasets of complexes is higher than that of state-of-the-art methods. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication specifies 'blind datasets' which clarifies the evaluation setting while preserving the substantive generalization claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Taking into account the evolutionary constraints on residues contributes to the predictions made by DLA. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Taking into account the evolutionary constraints on residues contributes to the predictions made by DLA. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The role of evolutionary constraints is stated identically in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: DLA serves as a general framework for transferring knowledge from available non-redundant complex protein structures to various tasks. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: DLA serves as a general framework for transferring knowledge from available non-redundant complex protein structures to various tasks. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the framing of DLA as a general transferable framework.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We present a robust learning framework for leveraging saturation mutagenesis experiments to construct accurate computational predictors of proteome-wide missense variant pathogenicity. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: We achieve state-of-the-art performance on clinical variant interpretation for unseen proteins across the human proteome using cross-protein transfer models trained on deep mutational scanning data from only five proteins. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication converts the framework claim into a more specific performance claim (state-of-the-art clinical interpretation across the proteome), increasing specificity and impact.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: CPT-1 improves specificity to 68% at 95% sensitivity of detecting human disease variants annotated in ClinVar. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: CPT-1 improves specificity to 68% at 95% sensitivity of detecting human disease variants annotated in ClinVar, compared to 27% for ESM-1v and 55% for EVE. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The numeric performance claim is preserved but the publication adds explicit comparisons to other methods, clarifying relative performance.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: CPT-1 compares favorably with REVEL for genes not used to train the method. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: CPT-1 compares favorably with REVEL for genes not used to train the supervised method widely used by clinicians. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the favorable-comparison claim and adds a brief descriptor of REVEL's clinical usage, without altering substance.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Vertebrate alignments provide a strong signal for variant pathogenicity prediction that is complementary to recent deep learning-based models. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Vertebrate alignments provide a strong signal for variant pathogenicity prediction that is complementary to recent deep learning-based models. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The claim about the value of vertebrate alignments is preserved in the publication with similar wording and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We release predictions for all possible missense variants in 90% of human genes. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: We release predictions for all possible missense variants in 90% of human genes. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The release-of-predictions claim is stated identically in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The developed model-based compressed sensing algorithm significantly increases the rate at which the existence and strength of synapses are screened. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A model-based compressed sensing algorithm was developed that identifies connections from postsynaptic responses evoked by stimulating many neurons at once. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication focuses on the algorithm's ability to identify connections (clarifying the mechanism) whereas the preprint emphasized the throughput increase; the method-to-effect link is preserved though the wording is more methodological.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model-based approach, which incorporates known biophysics of optogenetic mapping experiments, is critical for accurately determining synaptic connectivity. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A model-based compressed sensing algorithm was developed that identifies connections from postsynaptic responses evoked by stimulating many neurons at once. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication describes the developed model-based algorithm but tones down the evaluative language ('critical') in favor of a neutral description of capability, reducing the strength of the original claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The deep learning method developed allows for rapid switching between ensembles during stimulation without waiting for the postsynaptic response to return to baseline. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A deep-learning method was developed that isolates the postsynaptic response to each stimulus, allowing rapid switching between ensembles. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same capability (isolating responses to allow rapid switching) with essentially the same meaning and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The system increases the throughput of monosynaptic connectivity mapping by an order of magnitude over existing approaches. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The system increases the throughput of connectivity mapping by an order of magnitude. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The throughput-by-order-of-magnitude result is preserved verbatim in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The approach enables the acquisition of connectivity maps at speeds necessary to discover the synaptic circuitry implementing neural computations. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The developed computational tools enable learning of monosynaptic connectivity from high-speed holographic ensemble stimulation. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication frames the claim more concretely (learning monosynaptic connectivity from high-speed stimulation) and states it with higher certainty, making the preprint's aspirational conclusion more concrete.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: DNA methylation plays an important role in the adaptation of stress response mechanisms in plants. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: DNA methylation plays an important role in the adaptation of stress response mechanisms in plants. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The central biological conclusion about methylation's role in stress adaptation is stated identically in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Recent advances in long-read sequencing technologies allow for real-time detection of methylation. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Recent advances in long-read sequencing technologies allow for real-time detection of methylation. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the methodological/technological contribution claim verbatim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Methylation profiles for two maple species were generated across the full range of 5mC sequence contexts using Oxford Nanopore Technologies long-reads. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Methylation distributions were examined at high resolution across gene and repeat density. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication emphasizes high-resolution examination of methylation distributions (implying profile generation) but omits the explicit species/ONT phrasing in the summarized claims, so the preprint's result is framed more generally.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Examination of the transposable element landscape revealed the dominance of LTR Copia elements and associated patterns of methylation. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The examination of the transposable element landscape revealed the dominance of LTR Copia elements. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the TE-dominance result but drops explicit mention of the 'associated patterns of methylation', narrowing the stated findings compared to the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Methylation distributions were examined at high resolution across gene and repeat density within the broader angiosperm context. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Methylation distributions were examined at high resolution across gene and repeat density. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the high-resolution distributional analysis but omits explicit reference to the 'broader angiosperm context', narrowing the contextual scope.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Methylation in contexts other than CG is important to quantify, as it is extensively leveraged in plants. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: This explicit general conclusion about non-CG methylation importance is not present among the preprint claims and appears as an added emphasis in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: GETM demonstrates overall superior performance in imputing missing conditions and medications compared to existing methods. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: GETM demonstrates good imputation performance compared to existing methods. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication presents the same evaluation but softens wording from "overall superior" to "good" performance, reducing the asserted strength of the result while preserving meaning.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: GETM accurately predicts the status of chronic musculoskeletal (CMK) pain, chronic pain by body-site, and non-specific chronic pain using past conditions and medications. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: GETM-inferred phenotypes accurately predict the status of chronic musculoskeletal (CMK) pain. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains accurate prediction for CMK pain but omits the broader set of pain phenotypes claimed in the preprint, narrowing the claim to CMK pain only.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our analyses revealed the surprising predominance of medications and conditions in the cardiovascular category among the most predictive topics across chronic pain phenotypes. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Medications and conditions in the cardiovascular category are enriched among the most predictive topics of chronic pain. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication expresses the same observation using slightly different wording ("enriched" vs "surprising predominance") without changing scope or confidence.

alignments.pub_claim_text: GETM reveals known pain-related topics. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: This claim appears in the publication but was not present in the preprint; it is an added, related observation about recovered topics.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: AlphaFold2 (AF2) can model a transporter in multiple conformations to high accuracy. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: AlphaFold2 (AF2) has successfully modeled a transporter in multiple conformations to high accuracy. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same result with equivalent strength and scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Models generated using the default AF2 pipeline are conformationally homogeneous and nearly identical to one another. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication does not report this observation about default-pipeline model homogeneity; the specific claim is omitted.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Reducing the depth of the input multiple sequence alignments (MSAs) by stochastic subsampling led to the generation of accurate models in multiple conformations. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Reducing the depth of the input multiple sequence alignments by stochastic subsampling led to the generation of accurate models in multiple conformations. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication and preprint present the same methodological finding with the same emphasis.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The conformations generated in the benchmark spanned the range between two experimental structures of interest, with models at the extremes being among the most accurate. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Models at the extremes of the conformational distributions observed in the benchmark were among the most accurate, with an average template modeling score of 0.94. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the same observation but adds a numeric metric (TM-score=0.94) and reports it with higher confidence, amplifying the claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: There is a need for the next generation of deep learning algorithms to be designed to predict ensembles of biophysically relevant states. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The results highlight the need for the next generation of deep learning algorithms to be designed to predict ensembles of biophysically relevant states. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Both versions draw the same conclusion about future algorithm design with equivalent emphasis.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model quality was higher and the RMSD lower between AlphaFold and RoseTTAFold models for domains assigned to CATH families compared to those assigned to Pfam families or not assigned at all. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The model quality was higher and the Root mean square deviation (RMSD) lower between AlphaFold and RoseTTAFold models for domains assigned to CATH families compared to those assigned to Pfam families or not assigned at all. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this quantitative comparison with equivalent wording and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We could explain 80% of disease-associated mutations based on their proximity to functional sites, structural destabilization, or pathogenicity. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: We could explain 80% of disease-associated mutations based on their proximity to functional sites, structural destabilization, or pathogenicity. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The claim is carried over identically into the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A larger percentage of disease-associated missense mutations were found to be buried, closer to predicted functional sites, and predicted as destabilizing and/or pathogenic compared to polymorphisms. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A larger percentage of disease-associated missense mutations were found to be buried, closer to predicted functional sites, and predicted as destabilizing and pathogenic when compared to polymorphisms. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication tightens the logical conjunction (from "and/or" to "and"), making the criterion slightly stricter while keeping the same general finding.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The usage of models from both RoseTTAFold and AlphaFold provides better confidence in predictions, explaining 93 additional mutations based on RoseTTAFold models. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Usage of models from both RoseTTAFold and AlphaFold provides better confidence in predictions, explaining 93 additional mutations based on RoseTTAFold models. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication carries this conclusion forward without substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The crystal structure of VACV E2 was solved to 2.3 Å resolution, revealing two domains with novel folds. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The crystal structure of VACV E2 was solved to 2.3 Å resolution, revealing two domains with novel folds. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the structural result identically.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The C-terminal head domain of VACV E2 displays weak structural homology with cellular (pseudo)kinases but is not enzymatically active. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The C-terminal head domain of VACV E2 displays weak structural homology with cellular (pseudo)kinases but lacks conserved surface residues or kinase features. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication replaces the stronger functional assertion of enzymatic inactivity with a more cautious structural observation about lacking conserved residues/features, reducing confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: AlphaFold2 successfully predicts the structures of the head and ring domains of VACV E2 with high and moderate accuracy, respectively. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: AlphaFold2 succeeds in predicting the structures of the head and ring domains of VACV E2 with high and moderate accuracy, respectively. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the predictive performance claim with equivalent emphasis.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The advent of highly accurate virus structure prediction represents a significant advancement in structural virology. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The advent of highly accurate virus structure prediction marks a step-change in structural virology. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication strengthens the conclusion ("step-change") and raises certainty from medium to high, amplifying the claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: One-shot learning models could be used for predicting ligand-protein interaction. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: One-shot learning could be used for predicting ligand-protein interactions. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the same proposition about applicability of one-shot learning with similar emphasis.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The models attain better performance when protein targets contain conserved binding pockets. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The models attain better performance when protein targets contain conserved binding pockets. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The claim is carried forward identically.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A large number of drug excipients could interact with biological targets and influence their function. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A large number of drug excipients could interact with biological targets and influence their function. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this observation without change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: One-shot learning models can be used to make accurate predictions for excipient-protein interactions. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: One-shot learning can be used to make accurate predictions for excipient-protein interactions. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The conclusion about predictive accuracy for excipient-protein interactions is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: These methods could be used for selecting excipients with limited drug-protein interactions. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: These methods could be used for selecting excipients with limited drug-protein interactions. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication carries this proposed application forward without modification.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our predictions improved genomic prediction for fitness-related traits (grain yield) in elite maize panels by +5% and +38% prediction accuracy within and across panels, respectively. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: It improves genomic prediction for fitness-related traits such as grain yield, in elite maize panels, by stringent prioritization of fewer than 1% of single-site variants. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Both claims report improved genomic prediction for grain yield in elite maize panels; the publication adds the detail that improvement occurs via stringent prioritization of <1% variants, narrowing how the effect is achieved.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Predicting nucleotide conservation across Angiosperms may effectively prioritize sites most likely to impact fitness-related traits in crops. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Predicting nucleotide conservation across angiosperms may effectively prioritize sites most likely to impact fitness-related traits in crops. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication claim is essentially identical in wording and meaning to the preprint claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our approach, Prediction of mutation Impact by Calibrated Nucleotide Conservation (PICNC), could be useful to select polymorphisms for accurate genomic prediction. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: It improves genomic prediction for fitness-related traits such as grain yield, in elite maize panels, by stringent prioritization of fewer than 1% of single-site variants. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication provides concrete evidence that prioritization (the PICNC approach) leads to improved genomic prediction via stringent selection of <1% variants, clarifying and narrowing the original, more general claim about usefulness.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We annotated non-synonymous mutations in 25,824 maize gene models using genomic annotations and deep learning. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: No publication claim in the provided list reiterates the specific annotation count or the stated use of deep learning for annotating 25,824 gene models, so this specific result appears absent from the publication claims.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We obtain a total of just under 9,100 draft genomes, which collapse to around 3,100 non-redundant genomic clusters. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: We generated a total of just under 9,100 draft genomes, which collapse to around 3,100 non-redundant genomic clusters. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same numerical result about draft genomes and clusters with no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Co-assembly offers clear advantages over single-sample assembly in relation to MAG yield and quality. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Co-assembly may offer advantages over single-sample assembly for metagenome data from closely sampled longitudinal study designs. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication qualifies and narrows the preprint's general advantage claim to specific longitudinal study designs and downgrades certainty (may/medium), hence clarified and tempered.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Around 1000 MAGs were candidates for being considered high quality, based on single-copy marker gene occurrence statistics. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Around 1,000 MAGs were identified as high quality candidates, but only 58 MAGs met the MIMAG criteria for high quality draft genomes. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same approximate count of ~1,000 high-quality candidates; the additional MIMAG detail complements but does not change the original claim about candidate count.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Only 58 MAG formally meet the MIMAG criteria for being high quality draft genomes. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Around 1,000 MAGs were identified as high quality candidates, but only 58 MAGs met the MIMAG criteria for high quality draft genomes. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Both preprint and publication state the same concrete result (58 MAGs meeting MIMAG), unchanged in meaning or scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: These findings carry broader implications for performing genome-resolved metagenomics on highly complex communities. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The findings have broader implications for genome-resolved metagenomics on complex communities and the design of genome recoverability strategies. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication expands on the nature of the broader implications (adding design of recoverability strategies), which clarifies but does not reverse or narrow the original conclusion.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The BiG-DRP and BiG-DRP+ models significantly improve drug response prediction accuracy by leveraging drug similarities in terms of cell line responses and chemical structures. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The models leverage sensitive and resistant cell line information to form drug representations that enhance prediction accuracy. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication focuses on the cell-line response aspect of drug similarity to explain improved accuracy, clarifying the mechanism described in the preprint (which also mentioned chemical structure).

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Incorporating a bipartite graph in the models enhances prediction performance compared to other state-of-the-art models. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The BiG-DRP and BiG-DRP+ models significantly improve drug response prediction performance by incorporating a bipartite graph of drug and cell line relationships. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Both claims attribute improved performance to the incorporation of a bipartite graph of drug–cell line relationships with consistent emphasis and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The genes identified by the models that contribute significantly to performance are linked to important biological processes and signaling pathways. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: No publication claim in the provided list refers to gene-level biological process linkages; this specific conclusion appears absent from the publication claims.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model reveals important associations between mutations and drug sensitivity in patients' tumors, demonstrating its utility in pharmacogenomics studies. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Analysis of predicted drug responses reveals important associations between mutations and drug sensitivity, demonstrating the model's utility in pharmacogenomics studies. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the core claim of mutation–drug sensitivity associations and utility for pharmacogenomics, but presents it with reduced certainty (high -> medium).

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A dynamic programming algorithm was introduced to update the reference sequence according to previously aligned reads. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A new algorithm was introduced to update the reference sequence according to previously aligned reads. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same contribution (an algorithm for updating the reference from aligned reads) with equivalent scope and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed method allows for more reads to be aligned and produces assemblies of length comparable to the truth while limiting error rate. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed method allows for more reads to be aligned and produces assemblies of length comparable to the truth while limiting error rate. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same performance claims for read alignment and assembly quality without substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The method successfully assembled the first full mitochondrial genome for the western-grey kangaroo. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: None of the publication claims provided explicitly report the specific empirical result of assembling the western-grey kangaroo mitochondrial genome, so this specific claim appears absent in the listed publication claims.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The core algorithm of this method could be improved and combined with other approaches to analyze larger genomic sequences. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The current form of the method is not portable to genomic scale, but several improvements were suggested to enhance its flexibility for large genome assemblies. (certainty=medium, type=limitation)
alignments.rationale: The publication frames the same idea as a concrete limitation (not portable to genomic scale) and suggests improvements, which clarifies and elaborates the preprint's suggestion about future improvements.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The RNA, miRNA and methylation data of The Cancer Genome Atlas (TCGA) cohort of SKCM can be analyzed to recognize key genomic features that categorize various states of metastatic tumors from primary tumors with high precision. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The Support Vector Classification with Weight (SVC-W) model developed using the expression of 17 mRNAs achieved an Area under the Receiver Operating Characteristic (AUROC) curve of 0.95 and an accuracy of 89.47% on an independent validation dataset. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication reports a concrete high-precision classification result but specifically for a 17-mRNA SVC model, narrowing the preprint's broader statement about multi-omic TCGA data analysis to an mRNA-only model.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The SVC model developed using 17 mRNA features achieved maximum MCC 0.73 with sensitivity, specificity and accuracy of 89.19%, 90.48% and 89.47% respectively on an independent validation dataset. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The Support Vector Classification with Weight (SVC-W) model developed using the expression of 17 mRNAs achieved an Area under the Receiver Operating Characteristic (AUROC) curve of 0.95 and an accuracy of 89.47% on an independent validation dataset. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Both refer to the same 17-mRNA SVC model and the same accuracy; the publication replaces some reported metrics (MCC and detailed sensitivity/specificity) with AUROC, clarifying evaluation metrics.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Gene expression based features perform better than features obtained from miRNA profiling and epigenomic profiling in predicting melanoma states. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: No publication claim in the provided list explicitly states a modality comparison concluding gene expression outperforms miRNA and epigenomic features, so this comparative conclusion appears absent.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The expression of genes C7, MMP3, KRT14, KRT17, MASP1, and miRNA hsa-mir-205 and hsa-mir-203a are key genomic features that may substantially contribute to the oncogenesis of melanoma. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: This study reveals the genes C7, MMP3, KRT14, LOC642587, CASP7, S100A7 and miRNAs hsa-mir-205 and hsa-mir-203b as the key genomic features that may substantially contribute to the oncogenesis of melanoma. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The sets of nominated key genes and one miRNA differ between preprint and publication (several gene names and one miRNA variant changed), indicating a revision of the specific feature list while preserving the overall claim that specific features contribute to oncogenesis.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed computational framework successfully identifies potential therapeutic targets against pancreatic disorders. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed computational framework successfully identifies potential therapeutic targets against pancreatic disorders. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The texts are effectively identical in wording and meaning, asserting the same contribution with the same certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our methods demonstrated superiority over baseline methods in evaluating classification performance. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The evaluation from a classification perspective demonstrated the superiority of the proposed methods against the baseline. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Both statements present the same result about superiority in classification evaluation with equivalent certainty and scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: An enrichment analysis indicated that our methods achieved better outcomes compared to existing methods. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The enrichment analysis indicated that the proposed methods achieved better outcomes compared to existing methods. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The enrichment-analysis result is restated without material change in meaning or scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The results identified 44 unique genes and 10 unique transcription factors related to pancreatic islet functions and diseases. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The results for the three datasets included 44 unique genes and 10 unique transcription factors related to pancreatic islet functions, diseases, and therapeutic targets. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication adds that the results came from three datasets and extends the association to therapeutic targets, broadening and slightly clarifying the original finding.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: satmut_utils enables high-performance analysis of saturation mutagenesis data. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The satmut_utils package enables high-performance analysis of multiplexed assays of variant effect (MAVEs). (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication reframes the tool as enabling MAVEs (a broader/alternative descriptor) rather than specifically 'saturation mutagenesis', changing the stated target domain while preserving the contribution.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: satmut_utils achieves unprecedented specificity through novel error correction approaches. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication does not restate the claim about 'unprecedented specificity' or novel error-correction improvements; this specific assertion is omitted.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We identified an association between variants near chemical cofactor binding sites and decreased mRNA abundance. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: No corresponding result about variants near cofactor binding sites is present in the publication; the specific association is not reported.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We found a correlation between codon optimality and the magnitude of variant effects. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication does not report the codon-optimality correlation claim; this specific result is absent.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: satmut_utils reveals the capability of single-codon variants to alter mRNA abundance in native coding sequences. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The analysis reveals the capability of variants to alter mRNA abundance. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the core finding that variants can alter mRNA abundance but states it more generally (losing the explicit 'single-codon' and 'native coding sequences' detail), broadening the scope of the wording.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Existing MAVE software lacks standardization and does not scale to large targets. (certainty=high, type=limitation)
alignments.rationale: This limitation/benchmarking context appears in the publication but was not asserted in the preprint, so it is an added claim.

alignments.pub_claim_text: We benchmarked MAVE software using simulated and real MAVE data. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Benchmarking of MAVE software is presented in the publication but is not present in the preprint, making it an added claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: METAbolomics data Balancing with Over-sampling Algorithms (META-BOA) is a web-based application that allows users to select between four different methods for class balancing. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: METAbolomics data Balancing with Over-sampling Algorithms (META-BOA) is a web-based application that allows users to select from four different methods for class balancing. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same contribution with only minor wording change ('between' vs 'from'), preserving meaning.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: META-BOA generates a newly balanced dataset by creating additional samples in the minority class using various over-sampling techniques. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: META-BOA generates a newly balanced dataset by creating additional samples in the minority class using selected over-sampling techniques. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The result about generating balanced datasets via over-sampling is preserved without substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The application provides data visualization and classification to observe the augmentation effects of the selected over-sampling method. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The application provides data visualization and classification to observe the effects of over-sampling on the dataset. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication conveys the same functionality (visualization and classification to observe over-sampling effects) with equivalent scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Random forest classification is employed to compare sample classification in both the original and balanced datasets. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Random forest classification is employed to compare sample classification between the original and balanced datasets. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The methodological claim about using random forest for comparison is restated with equivalent meaning and scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Higher-order chromatin structures have functional impacts on gene regulation and cell identity determination. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication focuses on the EPICS method and related image-analysis findings but does not restate this broad biological conclusion about functional impacts, so the preprint's general claim is omitted.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We developed Extracting Physical-Characteristics from Images of Chromatin Structures (EPICS), a machine-learning based computational method for processing high-resolution chromatin 3D image data. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: EPICS is a machine-learning based computational method developed for processing high-resolution chromatin 3D image data. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the development of EPICS with equivalent wording and scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Using EPICS on images produced by 3D-EMISH or 3D-SIM techniques, we generated more direct 3D representations of higher-order chromatin structures. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Using EPICS, we generated more direct 3D representations of higher-order chromatin structures and identified major chromatin domains. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the representation result and additionally mentions identification of major chromatin domains; the claim is therefore clarified and slightly broadened.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We identified several high-contributing features from the model as the major physical characteristics that define the open or closed chromatin domains. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: EPICS demonstrates explainability and interpretability by identifying several high-contributing features that define the open or closed status of chromatin domains. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication emphasizes explainability/interpretability but preserves the identification of high-contributing features as defining chromatin domain status; meaning is clarified but not materially changed.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: EPICS can be applied to the analysis of other high-resolution 3D molecular imaging data for spatial genomics studies. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: EPICS can be applied to the analysis of other high-resolution 3D molecular imaging data for spatial genomics studies. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this applicability claim verbatim, preserving scope and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Hyperspectral reflectance data can classify plant growth-promoting bacteria inoculation status. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Hyperspectral reflectance data can classify plant growth‐promoting bacteria inoculation status. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same result about classification using hyperspectral reflectance with equivalent wording and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Phenomic prediction performs better than genomic prediction depending on the target phenotype. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Phenomic prediction performs better than genomic prediction depending on the target phenotype. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The conclusion about relative performance of phenomic vs genomic prediction is unchanged in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: AutoML is a promising approach for automating hyperparameter tuning for classification and prediction. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Automated machine learning is a promising approach for automating hyperparameter tuning for classification and prediction. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: Wording differs minimally ('AutoML' vs 'Automated machine learning') but the contribution and scope are the same.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Hyper-spectral prediction showed greater predictive ability for shoot dry mass and stalk diameter, whereas genomic prediction was better for plant height. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Hyperspectral prediction showed greater predictive ability for shoot dry mass and stalk diameter, whereas genomic prediction was better for plant height. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The comparative predictive-ability result is preserved verbatim in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The prediction models that simultaneously accommodated both hyperspectral and genomic data resulted in a predictive ability as high as that of phenomics or genomics alone. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The prediction models that simultaneously accommodated both hyperspectral and genomic data resulted in a predictive ability as high as that of phenomics or genomics alone. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The combined-model predictive-ability claim is identical between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: CD81 functions as a binding partner of CD44 and facilitates self-renewal of tumor initiating cells.
alignments.pub_claim_text: CD81 is a newly identified driver of breast cancer stemness and metastasis.
alignments.rationale: The publication frames the preprint's specific mechanistic result (CD81 binding CD44 and promoting self-renewal) as a broader conclusion that CD81 drives stemness and metastasis, thus clarifying and broadening the claim while asserting it with slightly stronger emphasis.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: CRISPR Cas9-mediated depletion of either CD44 or CD81 results in loss of both proteins in cancer cell-secreted exosomes, abolishing exosome-induced self-renewal of recipient cells for mammosphere formation.
alignments.pub_claim_text: In-depth analyses reveal that CD81 and CD44 deficiency alters endocytosis-related pathways and affects EV secretion and stemness-promoting functions.
alignments.rationale: The publication generalizes the mechanistic exosome observation to altered endocytosis/EV secretion and stemness functions; it presents the mechanism in broader pathway terms and with slightly lower stated certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Loss of CD81 inhibits tumor cluster aggregation, tumorigenesis, and lung metastasis of triple negative breast cancer (TNBC).
alignments.pub_claim_text: Membrane CD81 interacts with CD44 to promote tumor cell cluster formation and lung metastasis of triple negative breast cancer.
alignments.rationale: Both statements report that CD81 promotes cluster formation and lung metastasis in TNBC (publication emphasizes interaction with CD44), so the core result is preserved with equivalent certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: CD81 is expressed in over 80% of human circulating tumor cells (CTCs) and is specifically enriched in clustered CTCs from breast cancer patients.
alignments.pub_claim_text: CD81 and CD44 are coexpressed in human circulating tumor cells and are enriched in clustered CTCs that promote cancer stemness and metastasis.
alignments.rationale: The publication adds coexpression with CD44 and links enrichment in clustered CTCs to stemness and metastasis, thereby clarifying and broadening the original prevalence/enrichment claim without changing certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Nsp6 interacts with the endoplasmic reticulum membrane and is crucial for the autophagy pathway influenced by SARS-CoV-2 infection.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Nsp6 interacts with the endoplasmic reticulum membrane and influences autophagy during SARS-CoV-2 infection.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same interaction and its role in autophagy with essentially identical scope and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The mutation of Nsp6 in the Omicron variant suggests a possible different role of autophagy in this variant.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The mutation of Nsp6 in the Omicron variant suggests a possible different role of autophagy compared to other variants.
alignments.rationale: The published claim mirrors the preprint's conclusion about Omicron Nsp6 potentially altering autophagy, with equivalent framing and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The structural properties of Nsp6 were explored using long-time scale molecular dynamic simulations and machine learning analysis.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The structural properties of Nsp6 were explored using long-timescale molecular dynamics simulations and machine learning analysis.
alignments.rationale: Both versions describe the same methodological contribution (long-timescale MD and ML analyses) with no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The mutation in the Omicron variant may modify specific interactions, aiding in anchoring the viral protein to the lipid bilayer interface.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The mutation in the Omicron variant may modify specific interactions of Nsp6, aiding in anchoring the viral protein to the lipid bilayer interface.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the mechanistic observation about altered interactions and membrane anchoring with equivalent wording and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The P681R mutation in the spike protein of SARS-CoV-2 is responsible for increasing the infection rate.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The P681R mutation close to the furin cleavage site is identified as responsible for increasing the infection rate of the Delta variant.
alignments.rationale: The publication explicitly attributes increased infectivity to P681R, matching the preprint's causal claim with the same emphasis and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The mutations P681R and D614G reduce the amino acid-amino acid bond pairs (AABP) value, indicating reduced bonding cohesion between interacting residues.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The mutations P681R and D614G reduce the amino-acid-amino-acid bond pairs (AABP) value, implying reduced bonding cohesion and increased flexibility of the amino acids.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same quantitative AABP result and its interpretation about reduced cohesion and increased flexibility with similar certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The reduced bonding cohesion and increased flexibility of the mutated amino acids may contribute to the virus's ability to cause damage.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The mutations P681R and D614G reduce the amino-acid-amino-acid bond pairs (AABP) value, implying reduced bonding cohesion and increased flexibility of the amino acids.
alignments.rationale: The publication emphasizes the measured physical effects (reduced cohesion/increased flexibility) but is less explicit about downstream pathogenic consequences; however, the claim is reported with higher certainty, amplifying the confidence in the mechanistic observation.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The developed method for calculating AABP can quantitatively characterize interatomic interactions at atomic resolution.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The developed method of calculating AABP allows for a quantitative characterization of interatomic interactions at atomic resolution.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the methodological contribution but assigns it slightly lower certainty, so the claim content is unchanged but with tempered confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Using mutation quantifiers in a machine learning protocol could facilitate the prediction of emerging mutations.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The unique mutation quantifiers could potentially be used in a machine learning protocol to predict emerging mutations.
alignments.rationale: Both state the potential ML application; the publication frames it with somewhat higher stated applicability/certainty, amplifying confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The random forest machine learning method can accurately predict the host range of plasmids.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our random forest model can accurately distinguish between plasmid hosts despite the diverse nature and genetic plasticity of plasmids.
alignments.rationale: The publication presents the same core claim about accurate host-range prediction using random forests with equivalent framing and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The models achieved Matthews correlation coefficients of 0.662 and 0.867 at the species and order levels, respectively.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The developed method for predicting the host range of plasmids using random forests achieved Matthews correlation coefficients of 0.662 and 0.867 at the species and order levels, respectively.
alignments.rationale: The publication reproduces the reported performance metrics verbatim, so the claim is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Automated pattern detection methods improve the identification of plasmid host ranges compared to homology-based methods.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Identification of plasmid host ranges could be improved using automated pattern detection methods compared to homology-based methods.
alignments.rationale: Both assert that automated pattern detection outperforms homology-based methods for host-range identification with consistent scope and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Understanding the host range and dissemination trajectories of plasmids is critical for surveillance and prevention of antimicrobial resistance.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Detection of the host range of plasmids is crucial for understanding and predicting the dissemination trajectories of extrachromosomal genes and bacterial evolution.
alignments.rationale: The publication clarifies the broader evolutionary and dissemination implications (extrachromosomal genes and bacterial evolution) but preserves the original emphasis on surveillance importance.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The resulting high confidence network, termed ToxoNet, comprises 2,063 interactions connecting 652 proteins.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The combined high confidence network, ToxoNet, comprises 2,063 interactions connecting 652 proteins.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the network size and composition claim identically, preserving scope and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Clustering of ToxoNet identifies 93 protein complexes, predicting both novel complexes and new components for previously known complexes.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Clustering identifies 93 protein complexes within ToxoNet.
alignments.rationale: The publication concisely states the clustering result (93 complexes); the predictive interpretation about novel complexes/components is implied and preserved.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Clusters enriched in mitochondrial machinery include previously uncharacterized proteins that likely represent novel adaptations to oxidative phosphorylation.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Clusters enriched in mitochondrial machinery include previously uncharacterized proteins that likely represent novel adaptations to oxidative phosphorylation.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this conclusion verbatim, so the claim is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Complexes enriched in proteins localized to secretory organelles and the inner membrane complex predict additional novel components representing novel targets for detailed functional characterization.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Complexes enriched in proteins localized to secretory organelles and the inner membrane complex predict additional novel components representing novel targets for detailed functional characterization.
alignments.rationale: This claim is carried over into the publication without substantive change in content or certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: ToxoNet is presented as a publicly available resource with the expectation that it will help drive future hypotheses within the research community.
alignments.pub_claim_text: ToxoNet is presented as a publicly available resource expected to help drive future hypotheses within the research community.
alignments.rationale: The publication elevates the certainty of the resource's expected community impact (preprint low certainty to publication high certainty), so confidence is amplified while the claim content remains the same.
